info.title: Analytics
info.description:
The Analytics API is a RESTful API that offers fast, scalable, and reliable services for the analysis of financial products and portfolios based on current and historic market and portfolio data. It also supports the general output of this data. Furthermore, comparative values (so-called benchmarks), such as indices, can be analyzed. These functionalities are used by financial institutions particularly for the presentation of evaluation results and for internal and external reporting purposes.

One or several portfolios can be included in the calculation. The inclusion of open orders is also made possible. Open orders are orders to buy or sell financial products that were sent to the financial institution's trading system but whose trade has yet to be conducted. Besides analyzing already existing portfolios, individual financial products can also be combined and analyzed. This combination is known as a virtual portfolio.

In addition to the free selection of a unit to be analyzed, numerous other parameters are factored in. These include, for example, any points in time or periods of time in the past, the selection of a method of calculation, the inclusion of different currencies or the in- and exclusion of, for instance, fees, taxes and credits. This all increases the flexibility in calculation. The result can be output in different ways. For example, results may be aggregated on portfolio level or according to financial instruments. Other aggregation methods may include, for instance, asset classes, regions, countries and sectors.

The Analytics API provides two different types of resources for the computations it offers:

  • Synchronous resources, which provide immediate and complete results via HTTP, but should only be used for small numbers of portfolios.
  • Asynchronous resources, which provide an incremental stream of results via Kafka, and are intended to be used for large numbers of portfolios. These resources are solely to be found within the report section of the API.

tags.name: asset
tags.description: aixigo's asset API endpoints provide information on financial products and benchmarks, particularly  regarding asset master data and market data based on those financial products. The  resources are also used to analyze benchmarks and show various benchmark characteristics as well as the  composition of benchmarks. It is possible to select multiple assets or benchmarks for analysis. Please  note that the returned asset data is not bound to a holding context, such as a portfolio.

tags.name: portfolio
tags.description:
aixigo's portfolio API endpoints are used to conduct analyses on portfolio level. They provide information on security and liquidity holdings, initial costs, initial prices and initial currency prices, interests and maturities, transactions, underlyings and property proportions, as well as statistical values for holdings and transactions.

With the option of including individual positions or a selection of positions as well as a selection of modules in the calculation or excluding them from the calculation, it is possible to analyse just a subset of the given portfolio. Additionally, virtual portfolios and portfolio sets can be created.

tags.name: portfolio-kpi
tags.description:
aixigo's portfolio-kpi API endpoints calculate of key figures at portfolio level. Besides the attribution and contribution, this concerns key performance and risk indicators as well as key figures in respect of gains and losses. The calculation of specific bond characteristics is also possible, as is performing structural analyses.

With the option of including individual positions or a selection of positions as well as a selection of modules in the calculation or excluding them from the calculation, it is possible to analyse just a subset of the given portfolio. Additionally, virtual portfolios and portfolio sets can be created.

tags.name: report
tags.description: aixigo's report API endpoints are convenience resources that perform multiple KPI calculations at once and return a compound result with all the information in a single request. This can be used to  determine data and key figures for the creation of various reporting content structures. The  report can include a freely selectable analysis unit. It is thus possible to comprise information  about individual portfolios, portfolio sets and virtual portfolios and to in- and exclude portfolio  positions and modules. The report API endpoints also provide asynchronous resources which facilitate  the calculation of KPIs for large numbers of portfolios.

tags.name: data
tags.description: The data section of the API provides endpoints to retrieve some raw data (such as code values and asset properties), as well as to find out which cache data version is used in the current service instance. This might be necessary in certain workflow that rely on a consistent data view across multiple services.

tags.name: data-provisioning
tags.description: The data-provisioning endpoints can be used to create, change, or delete analytics data. This is meant as a REST-based alternative to the importer for writing small numbers of individual records. To write larger batches of data, the importer should be used.

get.description: The asset API groups those API endpoints that have one or more of the above-mentioned capabilities.  It also determines the path used to call the API endpoints.

The assets API endpoint shows all relevant master and market data and, if applicable, the  underlying structures of a selected asset on a specified date. The asset master data  includes the asset name, ISIN and WKN (Wertpapierkennnummer: German securities  identification code) as well as the issuer of the asset. The asset market data contains  the current asset price as well as the exchange rate and the base currency. If the  selected asset contains underlyings, such as funds or foreign exchange, the underlying  financial instruments are output with the inclusion of the asset proportion and a  selection of master data.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the identifier(s) for the asset(s) to be returned.

Each identifier can be either an asset ID, an ISIN, or a local ID. While ISINs are standardized, what exactly the asset ID and/or local ID represent depends on the data universe of the software installation. The local ID is typically some kind of regional or national asset identifier like the WKN in Germany. The asset ID might be the same as the ISIN or local ID, or it might be some other kind of bank-specific ID.

Each identifier can be chosen independently, so you could submit an asset ID for one asset, an ISIN for another, and a local ID for a third.

If no asset ID is given all known assets are used for the response.

Requested assets (either all or a specific list) can be paginated by parameters limit and page.

WARNING: Requesting all assets at once can be slow due to huge numbers!

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: additionalAssetData
parameters.description:
This parameter enables the inclusion of additional asset data in the output.

The following values are available:

  • ESG: Shows asset esg data, such as value, esgCategory ID, validFrom, validUntil, etc., for each asset.

Multi-selection is possible. The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies a quantity to assume for a specified asset.

Specifying a quantity is relevant only for the calculation of accrued interest rates, and only for assets with quantity-dependent interest rates. If you don't have assets with quantity-dependent interest rates, or don't care about accrued interest rates in general, you can safely ignore this parameter.

If you do specify quantities, you have to specify exactly one quantity for each specified asset, and in the same order. In other words, the first quantity you specify must apply to the first asset, the second quantity to the second asset, etc.

This also means that if you don't specify any assets (in order to get information on all assets), you must not specify quantities either.

If no quantities are specified, the interest rate with the largest maximum quantity is chosen for each asset that has quantity-dependent interest rates.

parameters.name: evaluateRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which properties from a property hierarchy are included in the result.

The following values are available:

  • true: Returns the root properties from a hierarchy. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property ROOT, the result includes ROOT.
  • false: Returns the properties that are directly assigned to the assets. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property ROOT, the result includes LEAF.
If a property hierarchy has multiple levels, any "intermediate" levels are ignored. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property STEM, which in turn has a parent property ROOT, then the result will include either ROOT or LEAF, depending on how the parameter is set, but it will never include STEM.

If properties do not have hierarchies, this parameter has no effect.

The default value is false, i.e. by default the result includes the properties that are directly assigned to the assets.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the maximum number of entries per page when using pagination. When limit is not defined or 0 pagination is disabled.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the page to be retrieved. Pagination starts at page 0. The number of entries in a page is given by the `limit` parameter. When page is not defined pagination is disabled.

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

get.description:
This endpoint shows some or all of the properties of one or more selected assets on a specified date.

If property hierarchies exist, the endpoint returns not just the properties that are directly connected to the selected assets, but the entire hierarchies to which these properties belong. These hierarchies are returned as nested lists, starting with the root element of each hierarchy.

If property hierarchies do not exist, the endpoint returns only the properties that are directly connected to the selected assets.

Note that property hierarchies are only treated as such by the software if each level of each hierarchy has its own unique property type, and if assets are directly connected only to properties at the lowest level of their respective hierarchies.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the identifier(s) for the asset(s) to be returned.

Each identifier can be either an asset ID, an ISIN, or a local ID. While ISINs are standardized, what exactly the asset ID and/or local ID represent depends on the data universe of the software installation. The local ID is typically some kind of regional or national asset identifier like the WKN in Germany. The asset ID might be the same as the ISIN or local ID, or it might be some other kind of bank-specific ID.

Each identifier can be chosen independently, so you could submit an asset ID for one asset, an ISIN for another, and a local ID for a third.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: hierarchyType
parameters.description:
This parameter restricts the result to properties that belong to the hierarchy of the given type.

The type of a hierarchy is the same as the property type of the hierarchy's root property. E.g., if you have a hierarchy consisting of property types REGION and COUNTRY, with REGION being the root property, then the hierarchy type of this hierarchy is REGION.

Note that this parameter requires consistent property hierarchies to exist, as defined in the endpoint's description. If consistent property hierarchies do not exist, this parameter has no effect.

parameters.name: propertyType
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the result to properties of the given type.

parameters.name: evaluateRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies whether the result should include the properties that are directly connected to the selected assets, or the associated root properties.

If the parameter is set to true, the result includes the root properties. Otherwise, it includes the properties that are directly connected to the selected assets.

The default value is false, i.e. by default the result includes the properties that are directly connected to the selected assets.

Note that this parameter has no effect if consistent property hierarchies exist, as defined in the endpoint's description, since in this case the result includes the entire hierarchies in any case.

get.description: The quotes API endpoint shows all relevant master and market data of a selected asset on a  specified date. The asset master data includes the asset name, the ISIN, the trading  day and the currency in which the asset was traded. The asset market data contains the  asset price and the exchange rate of the base currency, as well as the accrued interest rate. If neither a specific market nor a specific depository for the selected asset is given, the endpoint returns quotes for the respective default market of the asset.

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the identifier(s) for the asset(s) to be returned.

Each identifier can be either an asset ID, an ISIN, or a local ID. While ISINs are standardized, what exactly the asset ID and/or local ID represent depends on the data universe of the software installation. The local ID is typically some kind of regional or national asset identifier like the WKN in Germany. The asset ID might be the same as the ISIN or local ID, or it might be some other kind of bank-specific ID.

Each identifier can be chosen independently, so you could submit an asset ID for one asset, an ISIN for another, and a local ID for a third.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: market
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the trading center on which a security, currency and/or an  index is listed. Each trading center has a unique ID.

parameters.name: depository
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the depository of a security. A depository is a storage location that holds securities. Each depository is identified by a unique ID. The prerequisite for using this parameter is that a market prioritization is recorded. A market prioritization describes the assignment of a depository to a trading center with a certain priority. Based on these priorities, the best trading center, which can provide a price for the day in question, is used to evaluate a particular securities holding held in a specific depository.

If the parameter market is set, the parameter depository is ignored.

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance. The following values are available:

  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It  should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation  results. Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization  for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.  This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if   "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is  made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies a quantity to assume for a specified asset.

Specifying a quantity is relevant only for the calculation of accrued interest rates, and only for assets with quantity-dependent interest rates. If you don't have assets with quantity-dependent interest rates, or don't care about accrued interest rates in general, you can safely ignore this parameter.

If you do specify quantities, you have to specify exactly one quantity for each specified asset, and in the same order. In other words, the first quantity you specify must apply to the first asset, the second quantity to the second asset, etc.

If no quantities are specified, the interest rate with the largest maximum quantity is chosen for each asset that has quantity-dependent interest rates.

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

get.summary: Exchange rates on a specific date
get.description:
This endpoint returns the exchange rates for one or more currency pairs on a specific date.

The exchange rate that is returned for a given source and target currency should be interpreted as a factor that can be multiplied with a value in the source currency to get the equivalent value in the target currency. For example, if the source currency is EUR and the target currency is CHF, then the exchange rate that is returned matches the formula "1 EUR = exchangeRate CHF".

This behavior can be changed by setting a preference EXCHANGE_RATE_REPRESENTATION. Setting this preference to INVERSE causes the endpoint to invert exchange rates, so that in the example above, the exchange rate matches the formula "exchangeRate EUR = 1 CHF" instead. So effectively, this is the same as switching the source and target currencies with each other.

If an exchange rate between a given source and target currency can't be determined directly, the endpoint tries to determine an indirect exchange rate via the base currency. In other words, it tries to determine exchange rates between the source currency and the base currency, and between the base currency and the target currency, and then multiplies these to find an effective exchange rate between the source currency and the target currency.

If no exchange rate can be found for the given date exactly, the endpoint returns the most recent exchange rate from before that date. Note, however, that exchange rates are subject to the same validity restrictions as quotes. In other words, if you set the preference QUOTE_VALIDITY_DAYS to some nonzero value, then an exchange rate will be returned only if it is at most this much older (in days) than the given date.

If the end result is that no valid exchange rate exists for a given pair of source and target currency, this pair is left out of the endpoint's response. If no valid exchange rates exist for any of the given source and target currencies, the endpoint returns an empty response.

parameters.name: sourceCurrency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies one or more source currencies for which to determine an exchange rate.

The currency must be an existing code value of type PMS_CURRENCY, otherwise the endpoint returns an error.

For each source currency, you must specify a corresponding target currency.

parameters.name: targetCurrency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies one or more target currencies for which to determine an exchange rate.

The currency must be an existing code value of type PMS_CURRENCY, otherwise the endpoint returns an error.

For each target currency, you must specify a corresponding source currency.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the date for the exchange rate search. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

If you specify multiple pairs of source and target currencies, this date applies to all of them. To look up exchange rates on multiple dates, you have to call the endpoint multiple times.

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of exchange rates are requested from any exchange rate suppliers that may exist.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: Exchange rates are requested from suppliers for closing exchange rates.
  • REAL_TIME: Exchange rates are requested from suppliers for real-time exchange rates.
  • NEAR_TIME: Exchange rates are requested from suppliers for near-time exchange rates.
If nothing is selected, CLOSING is used.

If no exchange rate suppliers of the requested type are available, or if none of them return a valid exchange rate, the endpoint attempts to find exchange rates in the cache layer instead.

get.description:
The benchmark-performance API endpoint calculates the performance of one or more benchmarks over a specified time period. In addition to the benchmark performance, the annualized performance in the assessed period is shown. The underlying algorithm is the time-weighted return. This calculation of return enables the comparison of the benchmark performance to the performance of the regarded portfolios, as it does not include cash in- and outflows.

Due to different price developments of a benchmark's assets, the effective composition of a benchmark can shift. Comparing the performance of a benchmark to that of a portfolio therefore lacks accuracy. To tackle that issue, it is possible to specify a reallocation interval or a list of dates at which the benchmark is rebalanced so that it matches its predefined composition.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The  date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is  "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation  will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the  benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

parameters.name: reallocationInterval
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval in which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

If at least one reallocation date (reallocationDates) is specified, this value is ignored.

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to a monthly rebalancing of the benchmark.

parameters.name: reallocationDates
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the exact times at which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any number of dates can be specified.

get.description:
NOTE: This resource is deprecated. Please use /asset/benchmarks instead.

The benchmark-proportions API endpoint shows the composition of selected benchmarks over a specified time period. Benchmarks are always a composition of assets that were defined in the product universe.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The  date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is  "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation  will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the  benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

get.description: The resource returns each benchmark's ID, short name, and long name, as well as its benchmark proportions. It is possible to choose via query parameter whether benchmark proportions are returned as they are in the cache (possibly including references to other benchmarks), or resolved down to the asset level.  The default is to return them as they are in the cache.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The  date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is  "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation  will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the  benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

parameters.name: resolveAssets
parameters.description: This parameter specifies whether benchmark proportions will be resolved to assets. Default is false.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the maximum number of entries per page when using pagination. When limit is not defined or 0 pagination is disabled.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the page to be retrieved. Pagination starts at page 0. The number of entries in a page is given by the `limit` parameter. When page is not defined pagination is disabled.

get.description:
The benchmark-quote-series API endpoint calculates the daily returns of one or more benchmarks over a specified time period.

Since benchmarks are composed of different assets, it can be useful to see how the quote for an asset changed over time, and how these changes compare to shifts in quotes for other assets over the same time period.

Due to different price developments of a benchmark's assets, the effective composition of a benchmark can shift. Comparing the quote time series of a benchmark to that of a portfolio therefore lacks accuracy. To tackle that issue, it is possible to specify a reallocation interval or a list of dates at which the benchmark is rebalanced so that it matches its predefined composition.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The  date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is  "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation  will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the  benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

parameters.name: reallocationInterval
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval in which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

If at least one reallocation date (reallocationDates) is specified, this value is ignored.

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to a monthly rebalancing of the benchmark.

parameters.name: reallocationDates
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the exact times at which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any number of dates can be specified.

get.description:
The benchmark-risk-characteristics API endpoint calculates risk values of all selected benchmarks over a specified time period. Currently, the only risk value supported by this resource is the maximum drawdown.
  • Maximum drawdown: A drawdown is the relative loss between a maximum and the following minimum within a given period. The maximum drawdown is therefore the maximum loss that would occur within a period if the investor had invested at the peak and realized the loss at the subsequent trough of the investment.

To avoid distortions of the benchmark and portfolio due to different assessment dates, specific dates or time intervals can be set for the reallocation of the underlying benchmark or benchmarks.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The  date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is  "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation  will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the  benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

parameters.name: reallocationInterval
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval (in days) in which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

If at least one reallocation date (reallocationDates) is specified, this value is ignored.

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to a monthly rebalancing of the benchmark.

parameters.name: reallocationDates
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the exact times at which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any number of dates can be specified.

get.description:
This API endpoint contains an overview of all available data resources.

Data resources provide information about the cache layer that is specific to the analytics API or analytics-related caches.

get.summary: Currently used version of existing cache system
get.description:
As it can be important to know exactly which data the analytics API uses, the cache system ID can be entered here to get the version of the cache system that is currently being used.

This is typically the most recent version of this cache system, but may be an older one if the analytics API is still in the process of updating to the most recent version, or if it has been explicitly configured to use an older version.

parameters.name: cacheSystemId
parameters.description: The cache system ID

404.description: Unknown cache system id.

get.summary: All code values of a given type
get.description: This API endpoint returns all currently defined code values of the given type, sorted by sequence number.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The code value type

404.description: Unknown code value type

get.description:
This endpoint returns all currently defined property types.

If property hierarchies exist, the property types are grouped by hierarchy. In other words, the result contains one list of property types for each hierarchy, sorted from root to leaf property type. If property hierarchies do not exist, each property type is effectively treated as its own hierarchy, so the result contains each property type in a separate list with a single element.

Note that property hierarchies are only treated as such by the software if each level of each hierarchy has its own unique property type.

Also note that since property types are code values, you can also query all property types by calling /data/code-values/PMS_PROPERTY_TYPE, but doing so will not give you any information about property hierarchies.

get.summary: All asset properties of a given type
get.description:
This endpoint returns all currently defined asset properties of the given type.

If property hierarchies exist, the properties are returned as nested lists, with each property containing a list of its child properties. If property hierarchies do not exist, each property is returned separately, without child properties.

Note that property hierarchies are only treated as such by the software if each level of each hierarchy has its own unique property type.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The property type

404.description: Unknown property type

get.summary: All data to describe a contract
get.description: This API endpoint returns all cache data that is necessary to completely describe and analyze a contract. This includes positions and transactions, but also assets, quotes, exchange rates, and more. All data is serialized  in the default format and organized by cache ID.

parameters.name: contractId
parameters.description: The contract ID

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: A header to specify the desired output type, application/json as default

404.description: No contract available for the given contract id.

get.description: This API endpoint returns the selected preferences the software uses for the requested preference  keys. It is possible to specify a preference context and/or tenant to receive the selected  values used by the software if this specific context is set. It is also possible to request all values for the requested keys in addition to the selected value. While requesting preferences for an own tenant is always possible, requesting preferences from other tenants requires a special permission PMS_MAY_SEE_EVERY_PREFERENCE.

parameters.name: preferences
parameters.description: This parameter contains the preference keys of the requested preferences. If this parameter is empty, all preference keys are used.

parameters.name: tenant
parameters.description: This parameter is used to receive the selected values used by the software if the specific  tenant is set. This parameter can only be used with the necessary permission.

parameters.name: preferenceContext
parameters.description: This parameter is used to receive the selected values used by the software if the specific  context is set.

parameters.name: allValues
parameters.description: This parameter requests all values of the chosen preference keys in addition to the selected value. This parameter can only be used with the necessary permission.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the maximum number of entries per page when using pagination. When limit is not defined or 0 pagination is disabled.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the page to be retrieved. Pagination starts at page 0. The number of entries in a page is given by the `limit` parameter. When page is not defined pagination is disabled.

get.description: The portfolio API groups those API endpoints that have one or more of the above-mentioned  capabilities. It also determines the path used to call the API endpoints.

It calculates virtual transaction audit information for contracts. The contract IDs can be provided either by the inputTopic or specified in the request parameters contractIds or contractSet.
The results will be written in the kafka outputTopic.

get.description:
Returns information about pending orders for each holding. By default, the response only includes a flag for each holding indicating whether it has pending orders. To retrieve more detailed information, you can use the following query parameters:
showOrderDetails: Includes detailed order information such as the effective order ID,  associated orders, and transactions. showVirtualTransactions: Includes the virtual transaction that simulates the impact of the  pending order on the portfolio.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. The endpoint returns all open orders up to and including the specified date. More precisely, it includes all open orders that result in virtual transactions  with an impactDate less than or equal to the given when value.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

parameters.name: showOrderDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies whether the calculation basis of each pending order should be included in the response. The order details contain the effectiveOrderId, which is the identifier used by the pending order handling logic to generate the virtual transaction. Additionally, the orderDetails section includes the list of associated orders and transactions.

The following values are available:

  • true: The order details are shown
  • false: Hides the order details

Default value: false By default, order details are not included in the response.

parameters.name: showVirtualTransactions
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the virtual transaction generated from the pending order should be included in the response. The virtual transaction represents the simulated effect of the pending order on the portfolio.

The following values are available:

  • true: The virtual transaction is shown
  • false: Hides the virtual transaction

By default, the virtual transaction is not included in the response.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found.

get.description:
The assets-period API endpoint shows all relevant master data, market data, properties and, if applicable the underlying structure for assets of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period. The master data includes the name, ISIN, inception date and the security type. The market data contains the price in the base currency and various interest data for the end of the regarded time period. Furthermore, the proportion of an asset property (e.g., region, country, sector) is calculated. If an asset is a fund, derivative or foreign currency, the underlying financial instruments are output with the inclusion of the asset proportion and a selection of master data.

While the assets-period API endpoint identifies all the points in time when payments were made, the assets-snapshot API endpoint simply shows the moment of the last interest payment. The only difference between both API endpoints is thus the parameters that specify the time when the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is reviewed (begin, end and when). Beyond that, the two API endpoints are identical in use.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end  date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: restriction
parameters.description:
This parameter determines how financial instruments are included in the calculation. The restriction relates to the financial instruments that belong to the specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) or their properties that have changed in the regarded time period. It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).
