This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: externalValuations
parameters.description:
This parameter defines the output structure of so-called legal positions. Legal positions are the positions of a portfolio that are reported back from the core banking system.

The following values are available:

  • QUANTITIES: Outputs legal positions as quantities.
  • VALUES: Outputs legal positions as values.
  • QUANTITIES_AND_VALUES: Outputs legal positions as both.
If there is a valuation gap, i.e., a difference in quantities between the sum of all position-changing transactions and legal positions, the gap is output by the additional value externalQuantityGap. This value is only displayed if QUANTITIES or QUANTITIES_AND_VALUES were selected. If there is no valuation gap, the value of this field is 0. If there is no valuation available for comparison, the value of this field is null.

Note: Be aware that the calculation of valuation gaps is not compatible with the inclusion of pending orders, since the inclusion of pending orders leads to virtual transactions that do not have valuation counterparts. If you set the parameter includePendingOrders to true, all valuation gaps will be reported as null.

The values externalQuantity, externalQuantityGap and externalValue are only output if the aggregation method ASSET or POSITION is selected.

The default value is "NONE" (no output of legal positions).

parameters.name: useLatestValuations
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the delivered transactions on the query date should be compared against the legal positions on the query date or the current date.
  • true: Compares the legal positions of the current date.
  • false: Compares the legal positions of the query date.
The default value is "false" (comparison with legal position of the query date).

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.description: The value-series API endpoint determines the security and liquidity holdings that belong to a specific holding  context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period. If no price is available for a security on  the valuation date, either the most recent price is used, the security is considered unevaluated or  it is valued at zero, depending on the configuration. If an asset cannot be converted to the desired  currency, it is also valued at zero. In addition, the accrued interests for each date of the time  period as well as the respective sums of holdings and accrued interests can be requested.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end  date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: restriction
parameters.description:
This parameter determines how financial instruments are included in the calculation. The restriction relates to the financial instruments that belong to the specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) or their properties that have changed in the regarded time period. It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedTransactionDetailedType
parameters.description:
This parameter restricts the analysis to transactions with a specific detailed type, or a selection of detailed types. Not allowed if excluded transaction detailed types are also defined.

Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

parameters.name: excludedTransactionDetailedType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes transactions with a specific detailed type, or a selection of detailed types, from the analysis. Not allowed if selected transaction detailed types are also defined.

Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: showValues
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the output should contain holding values.
  • true: Outputs holding values.
  • false: Disregards holding values.
Out of the parameters showValues, showAccruedInterests or showSums, at least one must be set to true.

The default value is true (output holding values).

parameters.name: showAccruedInterests
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the output should contain the accrued interest.
  • true: Outputs the accrued interest.
  • false: Disregards the accrued interest.
Out of the parameters showValues, showAccruedInterests or showSums, at least one must be set to true.

The default value is false (disregard the accrued interest).

parameters.name: showSums
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the output should contain the sum of holding values and accrued interest.
  • true: Outputs the sum.
  • false: Disregards the sum.
Out of the parameters showValues, showAccruedInterests or showSums, at least one must be set to true.

The default value is false (disregard the sum).

parameters.name: showTransactions
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the output should contain a separate time series of transaction data.
  • true: Outputs the transaction series.
  • false: Disregards the transaction series.
The default value is false (disregard the transaction series).

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation of the transaction series.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: valueCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the calculation method for the value of a transaction when included in the transaction series.

The following values are available:

  • ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED: Determines the value of a transaction based on the data passed by the parameter actualAmount defined in the data model. It is the legal value of the transaction that is delivered from the financial institution's core banking system.
  • PRICE_BASED: Determines the value of a transaction based on the product of the purchased and sold quantities and the security price. Depending on the selection of the parameter paymentCalculation, the value of the transaction also increases by the value of the accrued interest claims and credits received or decreases by the value of the taxes and fees paid.
Ideally, the values of the transactions are the same regardless of whether ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED or PRICE_BASED is selected. However, this might not always be the case due to inaccuracies in the data supply.

Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: transactionRepresentation
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies how each transaction should be represented in the transaction series.

The following values are available:

  • COUNT: Each transaction is represented by a fixed value 1. In other words, the series contains the total number of transactions for each day.
  • VALUE: Each transaction is represented by its value. In other words, the series contains the total transaction value for each day.
Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

The default value is "-" (no selection). If no value is selected, the value COUNT is used automatically.

parameters.name: transactionTypes
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies either an individual transaction type or a related group of transaction types that should be included in the transaction series.

The following values are available:

  • COMMITTAL: Only transactions of type COMMITTAL are included.
  • DELIVERY: Only transactions of type DELIVERY are included.
  • PURCHASE: Only transactions of type PURCHASE are included.
  • SALE: Only transactions of type SALE are included.
  • CREDIT: Only transactions of type CREDIT are included.
  • DEBIT: Only transactions of type DEBIT are included.
  • VALUE_INCREASING: Only transactions of tpe COMMITTAL or PURCHASE are included.
  • VALUE_DECREASING: Only transactions of type DELIVERY or SALE are included.
  • VALUE_CHANGING: Only transactions of type COMMITTAL, DELIVERY, PURCHASE or SALE are included.
  • VALUE_CONSERVING: Only transactions of type CREDIT or DEBIT are included.
  • ALL: Transactions of all types are included.
Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to &uot;true", otherwise it is ignored.

The default value is "-" (no selection). If no value is selected, the value ALL is used automatically.

parameters.name: transactionOrigin
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the origin of the transactions that should be included in the transaction series.

The following values are available:

  • EXTERNAL: Only external transactions (those that add something to the holding from the outside, or take something away from the holding) are included. For example, deposits to and withdrawals from a settlement account are usually external transactions, but depending on how the holdings are aggregated, other transactions may be as well.
  • INTERNAL: Only internal transactions (those that are part of a move or conversion within the holding) are included. For example, purchases and sales together with their associated settlement transactions are internal transactions if the holdings are aggregated in such a way that both the purchase/sale transaction and the settlement transaction are part of the same holding.
  • ALL: All transactions (external and internal) are included.
Note that this parameter is only relevant if "showTransactions" is set to "true", otherwise it is ignored.

The default value is "-" (no selection). If no value is selected, the value ALL is used automatically.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.description: The value-statistics API endpoint gathers statistical information on security and liquidity holdings  of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified  time period. The calculated key figures are the holding's start and end value, and the accrued  interest, as well as their minimum, maximum and average values.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end  date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: restriction
parameters.description:
This parameter determines how financial instruments are included in the calculation. The restriction relates to the financial instruments that belong to the specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) or their properties that have changed in the regarded time period. It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.description:

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

get.description: The GET contractsets/{contractSetId} API endpoint shows the portfolio set assigned to the specified ID.  A portfolio set is a compilation of portfolios on the basis of which analyses or calculations  by other APIs are conducted. The retrieved data are name and description of the portfolio set,  creation time, last modification time, expiry date, lifetime, mutability and all included portfolios.

parameters.name: contractSetId
parameters.description: This parameter identifies the portfolio set assigned to the specified ID. The portfolio  set ID is either a predefined ID (if the portfolio set was imported with an ID) or generated  automatically by the system (if the portfolio set was created via API).

404.description: Contract Set could not be found

delete.description: The DELETE contractsets/{contractSetId} API endpoint deletes the portfolio set assigned to a  specified ID. A portfolio set is a compilation of portfolios on the basis of which  analyses or calculations by other APIs are conducted. The resource requires that a  portfolio set can only be deleted by the user who created it.

parameters.name: contractSetId
parameters.description: This parameter identifies the portfolio set assigned to the specified ID. The portfolio  set ID is either a predefined ID (if the portfolio set was imported with an ID) or generated  automatically by the system (if the portfolio set was created via API).

404.description: Contract Set could not be found

patch.description:
Existing portfolio sets can be changed using the PATCH contractsets/{contractSetId} API endpoint. A portfolio set is a compilation of portfolios on the basis of which analyses or calculations by other APIs are conducted.

Changes to a portfolio set can only be made by the user who created it, and only to those portfolio sets that were originally created as modifiable, i.e., with the status MUTABLE. The name and the description of the portfolio set can be changed, as well as the expiry date and lifetime. Additionally, portfolios can be added to or deleted from the set.

parameters.name: contractSetId
parameters.description: This parameter identifies the portfolio set assigned to the specified ID. The portfolio  set ID is either a predefined ID (if the portfolio set was imported with an ID) or generated  automatically by the system (if the portfolio set was created via API).

requestBody.description:
This parameter modifies a portfolio set.

The input structure of a portfolio set contains the following parameters:

  • name: The name of the portfolio set. If this differs from the existing name, the existing name will be overwritten.
  • description: The optional description of the portfolio set. If this differs from the existing description, the existing description will be overwritten.
  • expiryDuration: The lifetime duration of the portfolio set to be created in the format P(n)Y(n)M(n)DT(n)H(n)M(n)S as per ISO 8601. If the lifetime duration is to be, for example, 1 year and 1 second, this would have to be entered as P1Y0M0DT0H0M1S. If this differs from the existing lifetime duration, the existing duration will be overwritten. If a duration is not specified, despite the parameter lifeTime having the value FINITE, the expiry time is set to 5 minutes.
  • lifeTime: The configuration of the lifetime of the portfolio set. Possible values are FINITE (expires after the expiryDuration) or INFINITE (does not expire). This parameter cannot be overridden but is required to validate the parameter expiryDuration. If the selected value differs from the previous value, the API returns an error message.
  • addContractIds: The list of portfolios to be added to the portfolio set. These can be selected via their contract IDs in the following format: ["contractId1", "contractId2", "..."]. There is no minimum number of portfolios to add.
  • removeContractIDs: The list of portfolios to be removed from the portfolio set. These can be selected via their contract IDs in the following format: ["contractId1", "contractId2", "..."]. There is no minimum number of portfolios to remove.

404.description: Contract Set could not be found

get.description: The contract-sets API endpoint returns all existing portfolio sets created by the user.  A portfolio set is a compilation of portfolios on the basis of which analyses  or calculations by other APIs are conducted. The retrieved data are ID, name and description of the portfolio sets, creation time, last modification time,  expiry date, lifetime, mutability and all included portfolios.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

post.summary: Contract set creation
post.description: The POST contract-sets API endpoint enables the creation of a portfolio set. A portfolio  set is a compilation of portfolios on the basis of which analyses or calculations by other APIs are conducted. It can be composed of any number of existing or  virtual portfolios.

requestBody.description:
This parameter modifies a portfolio set.

The input structure of a portfolio set contains the following parameters:

  • name: A name for the portfolio set (optional).
  • description: A description for the portfolio set (optional).
  • expiryDuration: The lifetime duration of the portfolio set to be created in the format P(n)Y(n)M(n)DT(n)H(n)M(n)S as per ISO 8601. If the lifetime duration is to be, for example, 1 year and 1 second, this would have to be entered as P1Y0M0DT0H0M1S. If a duration is not specified, despite the parameter lifeTime having the value FINITE, the expiry time is set to 5 minutes.
  • lifeTime: The configuration of the lifetime of the portfolio set. Possible values are FINITE (expires after the expiryDuration) or INFINITE (does not expire). If this parameter is not set, the default value is INFINITE.
  • mutability: The mutability of the portfolio set to be created. Possible values are MUTABLE or IMMUTABLE. This parameter must be defined.
  • contractIds: The list of portfolios comprising the portfolio set. These can be selected via their contract IDs in the following format: ["contractId1", "contractId2", "..."]. There is no minimum number of portfolios in a portfolio set.

get.description: The portfolio-kpi API groups those API endpoints that have one or more of the above-mentioned  capabilities. It also determines the path used to call the API endpoints.

The attribution API endpoint analyzes a portfolio's performance against a particular benchmark (i.e., a comparative value such as an index) to evaluate whether the portfolio is out- or underperforming. Two models are used for calculating the performance attribution: (1) The Brinson-Fachler model and (2) the Brinson-Hood-Beebower model. Both are additive models that have weaknesses in financial mathematics compared to multiplicative attribution models, but generally prove themselves in practice.

The following attribution key figures are calculated:

  • Active return: Is calculated as the difference between the portfolio return and the benchmark return. This deviation is then further analyzed by determining its causes, i.e., identifying the so-called sources of success. The common approach is to break down the active return into at least two factors: the selection and the weighting effect.
  • Selection effect: Occurs when the portfolio manager receives a higher return on the portfolio due to a selection or weighting of securities that deviates from the benchmark. A positive selection effect occurs when the portfolio return from a specific segment (e.g., a specific asset class, sector or region) is greater than the return on the comparative value from the same segment as a result from the manager's smart decisions in selecting and weighting securities. A negative selection effect occurs when the return from a certain segment is lower than the return from the corresponding segment of the comparative value.
  • Weighting effect: Comprises the allocation effect and the currency effect. An allocation effect occurs when the decision to invest in a particular segment generates a return. The currency effect refers to fluctuations in returns as a result of changes in the exchange rate between the trading and the reporting currency. It should be noted that the attribution API does not yet provide the allocation effect and currency effect as output values. This is due to our assumption that the trading and the reporting currency are the same, and hence there is no currency effect. The weighting effect is therefore solely represented by the allocation effect.
  • Cross product: Results after deducting the weighting effect and the selection effect from the active return. This is the result from the interaction of the success components with each other and thus cannot be clearly assigned to the success of a single source. The cross product proves to be extremely critical in case of long-term consideration, as the cross product assumes highly unacceptable dimensions.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is  made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end  date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01"  must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will  then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

NOTE: This algorithm can currently only be used with aggregation by property type.

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description: This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation.  The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

parameters.name: benchmark
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the benchmark ID, which is either a predefined ID (if the benchmark was imported with an ID) or generated automatically by the system.

NOTE: The number of specified benchmarks must correspond to the number of contracts, as the first contract will be compared to the first benchmark, the second contract to the second benchmark, etc. If several contracts should be compared to a benchmark, the same benchmark must be entered as many times as there are contracts.

parameters.name: attributionMethod
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which algorithm is used to calculate the attribution. A selection can be made between the Brinson-Hood-Beebower model and the Brinson-Fachler model.

The main difference between those models is the definition and interpretation of the allocation effect:

  • Brinson Hood Beebower (BHB) model: A positive allocation effect occurs when the portfolio manager overweighted a segment that performed positively relative to the benchmark. A negative allocation effect occurs when the portfolio manager underweighted a segment that performed positively relative to the benchmark.
  • Brinson Fachler (BF) model: A positive allocation effect occurs when (1) a segment that outperforms the overall benchmark is overweighted or (2) a segment that underperforms the overall benchmark is underweighted. In all other cases the allocation effect is negative.

With the BHB model, the allocation affect is thus already positive if the portfolio manager overweighted a positively performing segment. With the BF model, however, the allocation effect is only positive if the overweighted segment also has a better overall performance, or the underweighted segment has an overall poorer performance, than the benchmark value as a whole. Simply put, the BF model makes it more difficult for the portfolio manager to do well than the BHB model.

parameters.name: reallocationInterval
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval in days in which the benchmark is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested. This serves to prevent distortions caused by different assessment dates of portfolio components and the benchmark (e.g., in case of umbrella funds, valuation differences are minimized by adjusting the benchmark).

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to 30 days or a monthly rebalancing of the benchmark.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling

  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

get.description: The average-holding-properties API endpoint generates the average holding properties of all  holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified  date. The calculation of a property's mean value only makes sense for those properties  where the characteristics of the property can be expressed in figures. This is the  case, for example, for security ratings. Ratings assess the creditworthiness of the  respective companies, states or countries that issue a financial product.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.description:

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.description:

parameters.description:

The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.description:

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

parameters.name: propertySource
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether results should be aggregated by properties differing from the values listed under PropertyType. These include currencies and maturities of assets, which are not stored as properties in the aixigo data model but as fixed units of the asset data structure.

The following values are available:

  • PROPERTY: Aggregates results by the property specified under propertyType.
  • ESG_CLASSIFICATION: Aggregates results by the esg category of the assets. If ESG_CLASSIFICATION is chosen, esgCategory is mandatory, and propertyType will be ignored. Also evaluateRootProperties parameter will be ignored and correspondingActualProperty will be empty in the response.
NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters propertySource and propertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for propertySource, the field propertyType must be filled.

parameters.name: propertyType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the property according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed. The selection of such properties can be configured.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: esgCategory
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the esg category according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters propertySource and esgCategory. If the value ESG_CLASSIFICATION was selected for propertySource, the field esgCategory must be filled.

parameters.name: averagePropertyMapping
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which the calculated average value of a property is assigned to a certain property value.

The following options can be selected here:

  • NONE: Calculates only the average value. No property value is assigned.
  • TO_NEAREST_HIGHER_VALUE:Assigns the calculated average value to the next higher property value.
  • TO_NEAREST_LOWER_VALUE: Assigns the calculated average value to the next lower property value.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: evaluateRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which properties from a property hierarchy are included in the result.

The following values are available:

  • true: Returns the root properties from a hierarchy. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property ROOT, the result includes ROOT.
  • false: Returns the properties that are directly assigned to the assets. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property ROOT, the result includes LEAF.
If a property hierarchy has multiple levels, any "intermediate" levels are ignored. E.g. if an asset is connected to a property LEAF, and this property has a parent property STEM, which in turn has a parent property ROOT, then the result will include either ROOT or LEAF, depending on how the parameter is set, but it will never include STEM.

If properties do not have hierarchies, this parameter has no effect.

The default value is false, i.e. by default the result includes the properties that are directly assigned to the assets.

If propertySource is set to something other than PROPERTY, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: algorithm
parameters.description:
This parameter analyzes the property structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on various algorithms. The respective algorithm determines the distribution of the property across the portfolio and the calculated value is then output for each property. The following algorithms are available:
  • VALUE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the total value of all assets assigned to this property. The total value of all these assets is also calculated.
  • QUANTITY: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the number of all assets assigned to this property. The total number of all asset units is also calculated.
  • INITIAL_COST: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the acquisition costs for all assets assigned to this property. In addition, the sum of all asset acquisition costs is calculated.
  • VALUE_CHANGE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio)by the absolute price gain of assets assigned to this property. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) must be defined for each project individually.
  • RELATIVE_VALUE_CHANGE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) by the relative price gain of assets assigned to this property. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the portfolio must be defined for each project individually.
  • VALUE_PLUS_ACCRUED_INTEREST:Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio)by the sum of the market value and accrued interest of assets assigned to this property. The total value including accrued interest of all these assets is also calculated.

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.
