In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: riskFreeInterestRate
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the risk-free interest rate with which the performance of the investments can be compared. An appropriate risk-free interest rate must de facto be determined for each situation under consideration individually. On the one hand, all interest rates apply to a specific currency, and on the other hand, the relevant investment period must be considered. Usually, an interest rate determined on the money market or capital market is selected as the interest rate, in this case the interest rate that the solvent debtor has to pay in a certain currency for a specific term.

The input format is a floating point number with a decimal point, for example `0.01` for a risk-free interest rate of 1%.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `RISK_FREE_INTEREST_RATE` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `0.0`.

parameters.name: dailyYieldTransformation
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies how to deal with the daily returns when calculating deviation.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Calculates deviation based on the portfolio data's relative change in value.
  • LOGARITHMIC: Calculates deviation based on the logarithmic change in value (constant interest). In this case, the number of interest periods tends towards infinity, i.e., the period of the individual interest period tends to 0.

Usually, interest is paid for certain periods of time: Interest is, for example, credited annually, biannually or quarterly. A constant interest rate, on the other hand, means interest is not paid in intervals, but continuously. Imagine, for example, that interest is calculated and credited every minute, which increases the capital to pay interest on the next minute; then imagine seconds or even smaller time units and it comes close to the constant interest rate.

As far as deviation is concerned, the calculation based on the logarithmic change in value is consequently the more accurate method, as a continuous distribution is assumed instead of the discrete calculation based on a sample.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `DAILY_YIELD_TRANSFORMATION` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `NONE`.

parameters.name: dailyInterestRateCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter defines how the daily interest rate is determined based on the annual interest rate.

The following values are available:

  • LINEAR: Divides the annual interest rate by the number of days.
  • EXPONENTIAL: Takes compound interest into account.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `DAILY_INTEREST_RATE_CALCULATION` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `"LINEAR"`.

parameters.name: numberOfVolatilityDays
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the number of days for which the deviation is calculated. A corresponding number of daily yields is then included in the calculation. Upon calculating the deviation, it is necessary to specify equidistant time intervals (equidistance: distance between two points on a scale is equal). It makes sense to use the equidistant period of trading days of a year as a basis. On average, the number of trading days per year is 250 (365 days minus the weekends and holidays). To determine the exact deviation, however, it must be drawn on the exact number of included return figures.

If the period under review exceeds the number of daily yields, only the most advanced days are included in the calculation. If the period is exceeded by the number of daily yields, the result is upscaled according to the desired number of daily yields by means of the square root law. If the annualized deviation should be calculated, the number of days of the considered year must be entered here, i.e., the value 365 for a normal year and the value 366 for a leap year.

The input format is an integer.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `NUMBER_OF_VOLATILITY_DAYS` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `250`.

parameters.name: numberOfDaysPerYear
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the number of days in a year on which the calculation of the annual deviation is based on. For the annual deviation, the daily return values are included based on the current day. On average, the number of trading days per year is 250 (365 days minus the weekends and holidays). To determine the exact deviation, however, it must be drawn on the exact number of included return figures.

The input format is an integer.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `NUMBER_OF_DAYS_PER_YEAR` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `250`.

parameters.name: confidenceLevel
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the confidence level on which the calculation of the value-at-risk is based. A confidence level of 99% to 95% is often chosen, i.e., the resulting value will not be exceeded with a probability of 99% or 95%.

The input format is a floating point number with a decimal point, for example `0.95` for a confidence level of 95%.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `CONFIDENCE_LEVEL` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `0.95`.

parameters.name: liquidationPeriod
parameters.description:
This parameter defines the period in days on the basis of which the value at risk is calculated. One speaks of the liquidation or holding period, which usually lasts for one to ten days. In case of funds or stocks in particular, very short liquidation periods such as one day can generally be assumed due to the high liquidity.

The input format is an integer.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `LIQUIDATION_PERIOD` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `1`.

parameters.name: excludeWeekends
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes daily returns of weekend days from the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • true: Excludes weekend days from the calculation.
  • false: Includes weekend days in the calculation.

The exclusion of weekend days makes sense because values do not change on the weekend due to stock markets being closed for trading. If the weekends' daily returns were to be included in the calculation, the result would be skewed.

Note that setting this value to true while keeping `numberOfVolatilityDays` unchanged effectively extends the calculation further into the past, since earlier dates have to be included to reach the required number of days.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `EXCLUDE_WEEKENDS` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `false` (weekends are included).

parameters.name: ewmaLambda
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the lambda value to use for EWMA volatility and value-at-risk calculations. The EWMA calculation is used for values of {@code 0 <= lambda < 1}. For a value of 1.0 the non-EWMA calculation is used. Note that for lambda -> 1 both calculations would yield the same result. If ewmaLambda is not specified, a value will be retrieved from the preferences using the key EWMA_LAMBDA; if that key is not set, a default value of 1.0 will be used and hence the non-EWMA calculation is used by default.

The input format is a floating point number with a decimal point, for example `0.9`.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `EWMA_LAMBDA` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `1.0`.

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.description: The risk-characteristics-snapshot API endpoint calculates the risk values of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date. These include the volatility (daily and annual) and the contribution to risk (unweighted and weighted). <ul> <li>Volatility: A measure of the daily price fluctuations of a holding. A higher volatility means that the prices of a holding fluctuate rapidly in a short time span, while a lower volatility means that the prices do not fluctuate dramatically. The higher the volatility of a holding, the greater the risk of price losses and the greater the chance of price gains. Note that while the volatility reported by this endpoint is based on historical prices, it only considers the holdings of the specified holding context on a single date. In this sense, it is a measure of current risk, whereas the volatility reported by the risk-characteristics endpoint (which includes historical holdings as well) is a measure of historical risk. <li>Annual volatility: Annualized version of the volatility, i.e. a measure of the price fluctuations over the course of a year. <li>Contribution to risk (CtR): A measure of how much or how little a particular holding contributes to the overall risk of the specified holding context. More specifically, this is a measure of how much the overall risk would change if the holding's portion of the holding context was increased. A higher CtR means that the holding is a bigger factor in the overall risk of the holding context, while a lower CtR means that the holding does not contribute much to the overall risk. Note that the CtR can also become negative, meaning that the holding actually lowers the overall risk of the holding context, e.g. because it serves to diversify a portfolio, or because it counteracts the risk incurred by a specific other position. <li>Weighted contribution to risk (weighted CtR): A version of the CtR that includes the holding values as weights for each holding. The major advantage of this variant is that the weighted CtRs of all holdings always sum up to 1, meaning that the weighted CtR can be interpreted as percentage. E.g. a weighted CtR of 0.05 for a holding can be interpreted to mean that this holding contributes 5% of the overall risk of the holding context. Note, however, that the weighted CtR can also become negative for holdings that lower the overall risk of the holding context.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: The date for which the request is made. Must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
A set of contracts to include in the calculation.

Contract sets can be defined using the contract set API. A contract set can contain any number of portfolios, and can be used as a shorthand when calling other endpoints like this one. Each contract set must have a unique ID, which must be entered here to identify the set.

Alternatively, there are two special contract sets that can be specified here:

  • ALL: The set of all known contracts.
  • NONE: An empty set. This is essentially the same as not setting this parameter.

NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the special set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

NOTE: Either this parameter or the parameter contract must be set, but not both. (As mentioned above, setting this parameter to NONE effectively counts as not setting it.)

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
One or more contracts to include in the calculation. Each contract must be specified via its ID.

NOTE: Either this parameter or the parameter contractSet must be set, but not both.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

If not set, calculations are made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: dailyYieldTransformation
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies how to deal with the daily returns when calculating the volatility.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Calculates the volatility based on relative price changes.
  • LOGARITHMIC: Calculates the volatility based on logarithmic price changes (constant interest). In this case, the number of interest periods tends towards infinity, i.e., the period of the individual interest period tends to 0.

Usually, interest is paid for certain periods of time: Interest is, for example, credited annually, biannually or quarterly. A constant interest rate, on the other hand, means interest is not paid in intervals, but continuously. Imagine, for example, that interest is calculated and credited every minute, which increases the capital to pay interest on the next minute; then imagine seconds or even smaller time units and it comes close to the constant interest rate.

As far as the volatility is concerned, the calculation based on the logarithmic change in value is consequently the more accurate method, as a continuous distribution is assumed instead of the discrete calculation based on a sample.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `DAILY_YIELD_TRANSFORMATION` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `NONE`.

parameters.name: numberOfVolatilityDays
parameters.description:
This parameter sets the number of days for which the volatility, and values that depend on the volatility like the CtR, are calculated. A corresponding number of daily yields is then included in the calculation, starting on the given calculation date and going backwards.

When calculating the volatility, it is necessary to specify equidistant time intervals (equidistance: distance between two points on a scale is equal). It makes sense to use the equidistant period of trading days of a year as a basis. On average, the number of trading days per year is 250 (365 days minus the weekends and holidays). To determine the exact volatility, however, it must be drawn on the exact number of included return figures.

The input format is an integer.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `NUMBER_OF_VOLATILITY_DAYS` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `250`.

parameters.name: numberOfDaysPerYear
parameters.description:
The number of days per year to assume for the calculation of the annual volatility.

The input format is an integer.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `NUMBER_OF_DAYS_PER_YEAR` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `250`.

parameters.name: excludeWeekends
parameters.description:
Whether or not to exclude the daily yields of weekend days from the calculation of the volatility, as well as values that depend on the volatility like the CtR.

The following values are available:

  • true: Excludes weekend days from the calculation.
  • false: Includes weekend days in the calculation.

The exclusion of weekend days makes sense because values do not change on the weekend due to stock markets being closed for trading. If the weekends' daily returns were to be included in the calculation, the result would be skewed.

Note that setting this value to true while keeping `numberOfVolatilityDays` unchanged effectively extends the calculation further into the past, since earlier dates have to be included to reach the required number of days.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `EXCLUDE_WEEKENDS` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `false` (weekends are included).

parameters.name: ewmaLambda
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the lambda value to use for EWMA volatility and value-at-risk calculations. The EWMA calculation is used for values of {@code 0 <= lambda < 1}. For a value of 1.0 the non-EWMA calculation is used. Note that for lambda -> 1 both calculations would yield the same result. If ewmaLambda is not specified, a value will be retrieved from the preferences using the key EWMA_LAMBDA; if that key is not set, a default value of 1.0 will be used and hence the non-EWMA calculation is used by default.

The input format is a floating point number with a decimal point, for example `0.9`.

The default value is determined by the value of preference `EWMA_LAMBDA` if the preference is set; otherwise the default value is `1.0`.

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If not set, CLOSING is used.

If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether pending orders are included in the calculation.

Pending orders are orders that are known to the system, for example because they were generated by the Rebalancing module, but for which transactions do not yet exist.

  • true: Pending orders are included in the calculation as virtual transactions.
  • false: Pending orders are not included in the calculation.

If not set, pending order are not included.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.summary: Holding structures for a time period (two property types)
get.description:
The two-dimensional-structure-period API endpoint shows the property structure of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period. In conjunction with the parameter algorithm, more specifically the value MODIFIED_DIETZ_PERFORMANCE, this API endpoint can be used to illustrate the performance contribution. As the name of the API endpoint indicates, this is a two-dimensional analysis, which means that the user can conduct the analysis based on the combination of two property types available for selection. The analysis of one property is conducted by means of the one-dimensional-structure-period API endpoint.

The four "structure API endpoints" (one-dimensional-structure-snapshot, one-dimensional-structure-period, two-dimensional-structure-snapshot, two-dimensional-structure-period) are basically very similar. All four API endpoints reflect the structure of a holding that belongs to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) in terms of its property proportions. The snapshot API endpoints calculate the structure based on the development of assets on a target date, while the period API endpoints display the results for a time period. This is the reason why different algorithms are used. The snapshot API endpoints are particularly useful for structural analysis, while the period API endpoints enable contribution analyses.

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made.  The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end  date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed,  "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01"  as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and  including 31.12.2019.

parameters.name: restriction
parameters.description:
This parameter determines how financial instruments are included in the calculation. The restriction relates to the financial instruments that belong to the specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) or their properties that have changed in the regarded time period. It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

parameters.name: firstPropertySource
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether results should be aggregated by properties differing from the values listed under firstPropertyType. These include currencies and maturities of assets, which are not stored as properties in the aixigo data model but as fixed units of the asset data structure.

The following values are available:

  • PROPERTY: Aggregates results by the property specified under firstPropertyType.
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Aggregates results by the currency of the assets.
  • ASSET_MATURITY: Aggregates results by the maturity date of the assets.
  • ESG_CLASSIFICATION: Aggregates results by the esg category of the assets.
NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstPropertyType must be filled.

parameters.name: firstPropertyType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the property according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed. The selection of such properties can be configured.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstPropertyType must be filled.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: firstEsgCategory
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the esg category according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstEsgCategory. If the value ESG_CLASSIFICATION was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstEsgCategory must be filled.

parameters.name: evaluateFirstRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the extent to which property hierarchies are included in the evaluation of the first property. These dependencies can be defined individually.

The following values are available:

  • true: Takes property hierarchies into account.
  • false: Ignores hierarchies within properties.
The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: secondPropertySource
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether results should be aggregated by properties differing from the values listed under secondPropertyType. These include currencies and maturities of assets, which are not stored as properties in the aixigo data model but as fixed units of the asset data structure.

The following values are available:

  • PROPERTY: Aggregates results by the property specified under secondPropertyType.
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Aggregates results by the currency of the assets.
  • ASSET_MATURITY: Aggregates results by the maturity date of the assets.
  • ESG_CLASSIFICATION: Aggregates results by the esg category of the assets.
NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondPropertyType must be filled.

parameters.name: secondPropertyType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the property according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed. The selection of such properties can be configured.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondPropertyType must be filled.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: secondEsgCategory
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the esg category according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondEsgCategory. If the value ESG_CLASSIFICATION was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondEsgCategory must be filled.

parameters.name: evaluateSecondRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the extent to which property hierarchies are included in the evaluation of the second property. These dependencies can be defined individually.

The following values are available:

  • true: Takes property hierarchies into account.
  • false: Ignores hierarchies within properties.
The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: algorithm
parameters.description:
This parameter analyzes the property structure of a portfolio based on various algorithms. The respective algorithm determines the distribution of the property across the portfolio and the calculated value is then output for each property.

The following algorithms are available:

  • AVERAGE_VALUE: Determines the property's distribution across the portfolio based on the average fixed capital in the assets assigned to this property. The total amount of fixed capital in these assets is also calculated. The fixed capital is the average capital invested, based on the specified time period, with payments being taken into account to the day. This is basically the denominator of the formula for calculating the Modified Dietz method.
  • TOTAL_RETURN: Determines the property's distribution across the portfolio based on the return on assets assigned to that property. In addition, the total return on these assets is calculated, i.e., the gain or loss obtained.
  • MODIFIED_DIETZ_PERFORMANCE: Determined the property's distribution across the portfolio by the Modified Dietz performance of the assets assigned to that property. The overall (Modified Dietz) performance of these assets is also calculated.

parameters.name: structureCalculationDate
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies whether the structural analysis is to be performed for the beginning or the end of the time period. Depending on the selection (BEGIN or END), an analysis is conducted for the specific date and then extrapolated over the specified period. If the properties of a financial instrument in the holding context change during the regarded period, this change is disregarded.

Financial instruments that change their property structure over time are, for example, funds. Economic developments may call for a change in the asset class distribution within the fund. Depending on the selection (BEGIN or END), only information for one property regarding the holding structure over the entire period is taken into account; solely portfolio changes are included in the analysis.

The default value is END.

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.summary: Holding structures for a key date (two property types)
get.description:
The two-dimensional-structure-snapshot API endpoint shows the property structure of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date. As the name of the API endpoint indicates, this is a two-dimensional analysis, which means that the user can conduct the analysis based on the combination of two property types available for selection. The analysis of one property is conducted by means of the one-dimensional-structure-snapshot API endpoint.

The four "structure API endpoints" (one-dimensional-structure-snapshot, one-dimensional-structure-period, two-dimensional-structure-snapshot, two-dimensional-structure-period) are basically very similar. All four API endpoints reflect the structure of a holding that belongs to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) in terms of its property proportions. The snapshot API endpoints calculate the structure based on the development of assets on a target date, while the period API endpoints display the results for a time period. This is the reason why different algorithms are used. The snapshot API endpoints are particularly useful for structural analysis, while the period API endpoints enable contribution analyses.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

parameters.name: firstPropertySource
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether results should be aggregated by properties differing from the values listed under firstPropertyType. These include currencies and maturities of assets, which are not stored as properties in the aixigo data model but as fixed units of the asset data structure.

The following values are available:

  • PROPERTY: Aggregates results by the property specified under firstPropertyType.
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Aggregates results by the currency of the assets.
  • ASSET_MATURITY: Aggregates results by the maturity date of the assets.
  • ESG_CLASSIFICATION: Aggregates results by the esg category of the assets.
NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstPropertyType must be filled.

parameters.name: firstPropertyType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the property according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed. The selection of such properties can be configured.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstPropertyType must be filled.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: firstEsgCategory
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the esg category according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters firstPropertySource and firstEsgCategory. If the value ESG_CLASSIFICATION was selected for firstPropertySource, the field firstEsgCategory must be filled.

parameters.name: evaluateFirstRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the extent to which property hierarchies are included in the evaluation of the first property. These dependencies can be defined individually.

The following values are available:

  • true: Takes property hierarchies into account.
  • false: Ignores hierarchies within properties.
The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: secondPropertySource
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether results should be aggregated by properties differing from the values listed under secondPropertyType. These include currencies and maturities of assets, which are not stored as properties in the aixigo data model but as fixed units of the asset data structure.

The following values are available:

  • PROPERTY: Aggregates results by the property specified under secondPropertyType.
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Aggregates results by the currency of the assets.
  • ASSET_MATURITY: Aggregates results by the maturity date of the assets.
  • ESG_CLASSIFICATION: Aggregates results by the esg category of the assets.
NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondPropertyType must be filled.

parameters.name: secondPropertyType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the property according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed. The selection of such properties can be configured.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondPropertyType. If no value ("-") or the value PROPERTY was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondPropertyType must be filled.

The default value is NONE.

parameters.name: secondEsgCategory
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the esg category according to which the structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is analyzed.

NOTE: There is a dependency between the parameters secondPropertySource and secondEsgCategory. If the value ESG_CLASSIFICATION was selected for secondPropertySource, the field secondEsgCategory must be filled.

parameters.name: evaluateSecondRootProperties
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the extent to which property hierarchies are included in the evaluation of the second property. These dependencies can be defined individually.

The following values are available:

  • true: Takes property hierarchies into account.
  • false: Ignores hierarchies within properties.
The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: algorithm
parameters.description:
This parameter analyzes the property structure of a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on various algorithms. The respective algorithm determines the distribution of the property across the portfolio and the calculated value is then output for each property. The following algorithms are available:
  • VALUE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the total value of all assets assigned to this property. The total value of all these assets is also calculated.
  • QUANTITY: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the number of all assets assigned to this property. The total number of all asset units is also calculated.
  • INITIAL_COST: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) based on the acquisition costs for all assets assigned to this property. In addition, the sum of all asset acquisition costs is calculated.
  • VALUE_CHANGE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio)by the absolute price gain of assets assigned to this property. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) must be defined for each project individually.
  • RELATIVE_VALUE_CHANGE: Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) by the relative price gain of assets assigned to this property. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the portfolio must be defined for each project individually.
  • VALUE_PLUS_ACCRUED_INTEREST:Determines the property's distribution across the holding context (e.g., a portfolio)by the sum of the market value and accrued interest of assets assigned to this property. The total value including accrued interest of all these assets is also calculated.

parameters.name: paymentCalculation
parameters.description:
