This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as interest payments, refunds, fees and taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
If not set, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

get.description: The report API endpoint groups those API endpoints that have one or more of the above-mentioned  capabilities. It also determines the path used to call the API endpoints.

get.summary: Overview of one or more contracts
get.description:
The contracts-overview API endpoint determines the initial value, the current value, valuation gaps, transaction and performance values as well as other key figures for a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date or for the beginning of the holding period until the valuation date, respectively.

Which values and key figures are taken into account can be decided on. The overview contains at least the initial values of all holdings and the values of the selected day. Additional key figures, such as valuation gaps, transaction data or performance can be added by setting the appropriate parameters. Furthermore, it is possibility to show these values or key figures both for the selected holdings separately (e.g., separately for different portfolios) and as an aggregated view of all holdings.

parameters.name: when
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be  entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: aggregation
parameters.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

parameters.name: propertyAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties.  The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: esgValueAggregation
parameters.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

parameters.name: contractSet
parameters.description:
This parameter determines which portfolio sets and portfolios are included in the calculation. Selecting ALL in the area contractSet means that the results concern all portfolios detected by the system.
NOTE: Large contract sets - in particular the contract set ALL - may require a lot of heap memory.

Using the contractSet API, a list of existing portfolios can be defined as a so-called contract set, therefore allowing a random number of portfolios to be collected and used with other APIs. The contract set is selected by entering the ID in the contractSet field.

Selecting NONE in conjunction with one or more contract ID(s) in the contract field calculates the result for the corresponding portfolio(s).

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which portfolio or portfolios are included in the calculation. The contract ID is the individual technical identifier for each portfolio.

NOTE: There is a dependency between this parameter and the parameter contractSet. If this field is used, the parameter contractSet must be set to NONE. This parameter is mandatory if no contractSet is used.

parameters.name: selectedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts. Not allowed if excluded accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAccount
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio account or a selection of portfolio accounts from the analysis. Not allowed if selected accounts are also defined.

In addition to the correct account ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified account and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAccount and excludedAccount, as accounts can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions. Not allowed if excluded positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedPosition
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio position or a selection of portfolio positions from the analysis. Not allowed if selected positions are also defined.

In addition to the correct position ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified position and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedPosition and excludedPosition, as positions can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedModule
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAsset
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: selectedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: excludedAssetType
parameters.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

parameters.name: currencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

parameters.name: quoteCurrencySelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the quote currency in which results for quote, exchange rate, initial price, and initial currency are shown.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.

If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the quote currency is the same as the currency in which the other results are calculated.

parameters.name: quoteCurrency
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the quote currency in which calculation results for quote, exchange rate, initial price, and initial currency are shown.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter quouteCurrencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both quoteCurrencySelection and quoteCurrency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter quoteCurrencySelection. If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the quote currency is the same as the currency in which the other results are calculated.

parameters.name: exchangeRateSelection
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the exchange rate selection in which comparative results for exchange rate and initial currency price are shown.

The following values are available:

  • QUOTE_CURRENCY_VS_BASE_CURRENCY: Shows the result in Quote currency vs Base currency.
  • QUOTE_CURRENCY_VS_HOLDING_CURRENCY: Shows the result in comparison of Quote Currency vs Holding Currency.

If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the exchange rate and initial currency price will be in the same currency in which the other results are calculated.

parameters.name: additionalKeyFigure
parameters.description:
This parameter enables the inclusion of the following additional key figures in the output:
  • VALUATION: Key figures to evaluate portfolios. Selecting this value will add information on inconsistent holdings. Inconsistent holdings occur when the holdings information of aixigo's system does not correspond to the holdings information of the core banking system.
    NOTE:There is a dependency between this parameter value and the parameter aggregation. VALUATION can only be selected in combination with the values ASSET and/or POSITION of the aggregation parameter. If the aggregation method is ASSET, existing valuations that are position-based are aggregated by asset accordingly. However, if the aggregation method is POSITION, asset-based valuations are not subdivided but instead treated as if they do not exist.

  • INVESTMENTS: Adds information on transactions that resulted in the respective holding. This information includes the transaction type, date of the transaction (i.e., the day on which the security was actually traded), quantities and value of the financial product purchased or sold. In addition, the data quality of the data that enables this information to be provided is shown.

  • RETURN_SINCE_BEGIN: Adds the following results to the output:
    Realized and unrealized
    - absolute price gain/loss (totalReturn)
    - accrued interest (accruedInterest)
    - price-based gain/loss (priceBasedReturn)
    - currency-based gain/loss (currencyBasedReturn)
    - credits (creditAdvices)
    - fees (fees)
    - taxes (taxes)
    The start of the period under consideration is the date of the first holding. Its end is the reference date specified under parameter when.

  • PERFORMANCE_SINCE_BEGIN: Calculates the Modified Dietz performance (based on weighted cash flows), money-weighted performance (based on the internal rate of return) and time-weighted performance (based on daily returns). The start of the period under consideration is the date of the first holding. Its end is the reference date specified under parameter when.
Multi-selection is possible. The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: additionalAssetData
parameters.description:
This parameter enables the inclusion of additional asset data in the output.

The following values are available:

  • BASIC_DATA: Shows basic asset data, such as ISIN, local ID, notation type, issue date, maturity date, etc., for each holding.

  • RELATIONSHIPS: Shows asset-to-asset relationships, such as underlyings or asset compositions, for each holding.

  • PROPERTIES: Shows all properties and property proportions that were available on the specified date for each holding.

  • QUOTE: Shows quotes and exchange rates that were available on the specified date for each holding.

  • INTEREST: Shows the interest rate on the specified date and the previously valid interest rate for each holding.

  • DIVIDEND: Shows the last dividend (per share) on or before the specified date for each holding.

  • DERIVATIVE: Shows derivatives with all risks that were available on the specified date for each holding.
NOTE:There is a dependency between the parameters additionalAssetData and aggregation. If one of the above-mentioned options was selected for additionalAssetData, the parameter aggregation must be filled with ASSET and/or POSITION.

Multi-selection is possible. The default value is "-" (no selection).

parameters.name: includeTotals
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether a total overview of the portfolio should be given in the output. If, for example, the aggregation method is POSITION because the output values are to be specified for each security position, specifying this parameter also adds the output values for the selected holding context, e.g., a specific portfolio. If several portfolios were selected for analysis, the algorithm calculates the output values for the so-called consolidated portfolio. A consolidated portfolio is, for example, the combination of two portfolios into one overall portfolio.

NOTE: There may be dependencies between the parameter includeTotals and other parameters. If, for example, the currency is selected with inconsistent parameter settings, all results are shown in the base currency. This can limit the comparability of the output results.

The following values are available:

  • true: Outputs the consolidated view of the portfolio.
  • false: Disregards consolidation.
The default value is false (disregard consolidated view).

parameters.name: showPositionDetails
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether the calculation should be based on position details (such as a fund's composition) rather than the positions themselves.

This decision applies to each position for which one of the following is true: It has one or more child positions, or its asset has an asset composition. Setting this parameter to true means that the holdings of the position itself are replaced with the holdings of its child positions, or with virtual holdings based on its asset composition, respectively. In practice, this means you will see results for a fund's composite assets rather than the fund itself, for example (provided that the fund's composition is known).

  • true: The calculation is based on position details, meaning that results are based on child positions and/or asset compositions rather than the positions themselves.
  • false: The calculation is based on the positions themselves.

The default value is determined by an associated preference SHOW_POSITION_DETAILS. If this preference is not set, the default is false (the calculation is based on the positions themselves).

parameters.name: includePendingOrders
parameters.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

parameters.name: assessmentType
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

parameters.name: unvaluedPositionHandling
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

404.description: A contract, asset or other necessary entity could not be found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations. The node adds an
overview over key values and information about the contract.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received on a kafka topic and decorates these contracts with contract overview details.

The contracts overview details are the initial value, the current value, valuation gaps, transaction and performance values as well as other key figures for a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date or for the beginning of the holding period until the valuation date, respectively.

Which values and key figures are taken into account can be decided on. The overview contains at least the initial values of all holdings and the values of the selected day. Additional key figures, such as valuation gaps, transaction data or performance can be added by setting the appropriate parameters. Furthermore, it is possibility to show these values or key figures both for the selected holdings separately (e.g., separately for different portfolios) and as an aggregated view of all holdings.

As usual, the results will be added to the result per holding and as a structure equivalent to the return structure of /portfolio/contracts-overview.

The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the contract overview calculation process
get.description:
Each request for the contract overview calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the calculation process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the contract kpi calculation was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the contract kpi calculation is currently being processed

DONE—the contract calculation is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the calculation, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract overview calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Snapshot KPI streaming request
post.description:
The POST contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream API triggers the calculation of initial and current values, accrued interest, average holding properties, valuation gaps as well as absolute and relative gain or loss for a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date or for the beginning of the holding period until the valuation date, respectively.

In particular, this streaming API enables the processing of large numbers of portfolios. The values and key figures to be determined are based on the selection of parameters. It is also possible to show these values or key figures both for the selected holdings separately (e.g., separately for different portfolios) and as an aggregated view of all holdings.

The list of portfolios for which the specified values are to be calculated is determined via the input topic of the streaming API. The structure of the input and output topic is identical, allowing multiple iterations of this resource using different parameters without further intervention. The same structure is used in the APIs contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream, contracts-kpi-period-stream, and contracts-kpi-time-series-stream, so that these three APIs can be executed in arbitrary order. After each run, the output topic is enriched with the results of the respective calculation.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the type and level of detail of information in the logs. Logs can be used to analyze problems or processes that occur. The following information can be recorded:
  • ERROR: System malfunctions with slight restrictions for (individual) users are captured.
  • WARN: System malfunctions without restrictions for users are captured.
  • INFO: System state and system activity are captured.
  • DEBUG: System state and system activity as well as additional details are captured.
  • TRACE: Detailed system activity is collected to track typical problems. In addition, detailed information on calculations is provided.

requestBody.description:
The input structure contains the following values:
  • requestId: Freely selectable attribute which can be used to track the request status via the corresponding GET resource. If no value is specified, a UUID is generated. The requestId must be unique.
  • workflowId: Optional attribute for tracking workflows across several asynchronous resources. This ID is added as a log tag and mostly meant to facilitate log analysis. If no value is specified, a default workflow ID may set by the streaming nodes, if such a mechanism is implemented.
  • inputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic from which to read the input messages. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • inputKey: The Kafka message key that identifies the message batch to be processed. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • outputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing results are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing results to Kafka (i.e., if the streaming node is not a data sink).
  • errorTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing errors are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing errors to Kafka (which is strongly recommended for all streaming nodes).
  • cacheSystemVersions: The versions of all relevant cache systems to be used while processing the input messages. Relevant only if the node's business logic relies on reading data from the cache layer. If not set, the node will use the most current cache system versions available. Note, however, that in this case different nodes in a workflow may operate on different data sets, if new cache system versions become available while the workflow is in progress.
  • preferenceContext: The preference context to use when reading configuration values that have been stored as preferences. Relevant only if the node's business logic does that, and if different preference contexts are in use.
  • timeOutInMinutes: Maximum waiting time in minutes, after which still ongoing calculations at the current node are stopped. Can be disabled by setting the value to zero.

In particular, the following values are part of the request section:

  • resultMapKey: Freely selectable attribute with which the results can be referenced. Especially if the same streaming node is passed several times, the individual results can be mapped to their respective parameter selection using the resultMapKey. A functional, descriptive name can be chosen for this identifier.
  • keyFigures: Selection of key figures to be determined. There may be dependencies between the chosen key figure(s) and the parameter aggregation. Some key figures can only be determined if the aggregation ASSET and/or POSITION is chosen. Multi-selection is possible.
    • QUANTITY: Number of asset units.
    • VALUE: Value of security and liquidity holdings.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST: Accrued interest.
    • INITIAL_COST: Total value of all transactions that contributed to the holding. Unless a different configuration was set, the observation period always begins with the first transaction of the holding on the date that is queried. Optionally, accrued interest, fees and taxes can be included in the initial cost.
    • INITIAL_PRICE: Average initial price, which is the initial cost divided by the quantity of units/nominal value of the regarded position. In the process, all quantities that have been bought since the initial transaction are aggregated. If sales occurred in the meantime, the quantities are reduced accordingly under the terms of the FIFO method, i.e., the oldest assets of a holding are always sold first.
    • INITIAL_CURRENCY_PRICE: Average initial currency price, which is the initial cost in the base currency divided by the initial cost in the specified currency. The currency can be selected using the API.
    • VALUATION_GAP: Difference between calculated position quantities and position quantities provided by the core banking system (legal positions). Note: Be aware that the calculation of valuation gaps is not compatible with the inclusion of pending orders, since the inclusion of pending orders leads to virtual transactions that do not have valuation counterparts. If you set the parameter includePendingOrders to true, all valuation gaps will be reported as null.
    • AVERAGE_HOLDING_PROPERTIES: Average holding properties. The calculation of a property's mean value only makes sense for those properties where the characteristics of the property can be expressed in figures. This is the case, for example, for security ratings. Ratings assess the creditworthiness of the respective companies, states or countries that issue a financial product.
    • VALUE_CHANGE: Absolute price gain. This equals the current holdings value reduced by all initial costs. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) must be defined for each project individually.
    • RELATIVE_VALUE_CHANGE: Relative price gain. This is the absolute price gain in relation to the initial costs of the regarded holdings. The reference date marking the beginning of the time period is the date on which the respective asset was purchased. The end of the period is the day specified under the parameter when. Financial instruments that were sold (completely) before the time period ends are not included in the calculation. If the same asset was purchased at different times, a longer price history is considered for older assets than for the more recent assets. If a financial instrument is partially sold, the logic (LIFO, FIFO, etc.) according to which the assets are sold and/or kept in the portfolio must be defined for each project individually.
    • DAILY_VOLATILITY: Volatility. The volatility calculation is based on historical prices leading up to the calculation date, but (in contrast to the period-based value) considers only the holdings on the calculation date itself.
    • ANNUAL_VOLATILITY: Annualized version of the volatility.
    • CONTRIBUTION_TO_RISK: Contribution to the contract's overall risk. More specifically, this number indicates how the contract's overall risk would change if the investment in a particular holding was increased. High values indicate a large contribution, lower values indicate a smaller contribution, whereas negative values indicate that a particular holding actually lowers the contract's overall risk.
    • WEIGHTED_CONTRIBUTION_TO_RISK:Weighted contribution to the contract's overall risk. As the contribution to risk, but weighted with the holdings' values on the calculation date. In contrast to the unweighted contributions, the weighted contributions sum up to one, and can therefore be interpreted as percentages ("this holding contributes 8% to the contract's overall risk"). Note, however, that the weighted contributions can become negative as well.
  • when: Date for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
  • aggregations:The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

    Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

    • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
    • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
    • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
    • POSITION: Position
    • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
    • ASSET: Asset
    • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
    • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
    • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
    • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
    • ACCOUNT: Account
    • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
    • MODULE: Module
    • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
    • CONTRACT: Portfolio
    • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)
  • propertyAggregations: The types of asset properties which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • esgValueAggregation: The types of ESG values which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedModules: The module(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculations. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedModules: The module(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssets: The asset(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssets: The asset(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • currencySelection: The logic to use when selecting a currency for each holding. By default, the base currency is used. The base currency is also used if the chosen logic doesn't lead to a unique currency for a given holding.
    • CONTRACT: The currency of the holding's contract.
    • ASSET: The currency of the holding's asset.
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: The currency of the holding's first transaction.
  • currency: The fixed currency to use for each holding. Ignored if the currencySelection parameter is set as well. By default, the base currency is used.
  • valueCalculation: The method to use for calculating transaction values. By default, ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED is used.
    • ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the actual amount.
    • PRICE_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the quantity and quote.
  • paymentCalculation: What kind(s) of payments to include in transaction values. By default, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.
    • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
    • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
    • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
    • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
  • includePendingOrders: Whether or not to include pending orders in the calculations. By default, pending orders are not included.
  • unvaluedPositionHandling:This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.
  • ewmaLambda: This parameter determines the lambda value to use for EWMA volatility and value-at-risk calculations. The EWMA calculation is used for values of {@code 0 <= lambda < 1}. For a value of 1.0 the non-EWMA calculation is used. Note that for lambda -> 1 both calculations would yield the same result. If ewmaLambda is not specified, a value will be retrieved from the preferences using the key EWMA_LAMBDA; if that key is not set, a default value of 1.0 will be used and hence the non-EWMA calculation is used by default.

    The following values are available:

    • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
    • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
    • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

get.summary: Snapshot KPI streaming request status call
get.description:
The GET contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream API determines the state of a specific request initiated by the POST API of the same name using a unique request ID. The following states exist:
  • WAITING: The request with the corresponding ID was initiated but is waiting to be processed.
  • PROCESSING: The request with the corresponding ID is currently being processed.
  • DONE: The request with the corresponding ID is finished. This is a final state.
  • FAILED: The request could not be processed. This is a final state.

NOTE: The outputs numberOfCompletedSteps and numberOfErrors are not supported anymore and thus always zero.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract kpi snapshot calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Period KPI streaming request
post.description:
The POST contracts-kpi-period-stream API triggers the calculation of various return, key performance and risk indicators for a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period.

In particular, this streaming API enables the processing of large numbers of portfolios. The values and key figures to be determined are based on the selection of parameters. It is also possible to show these values or key figures both for the selected holdings separately (e.g., separately for different portfolios) and as an aggregated view of all holdings.

The list of portfolios for which the specified values are to be calculated is determined via the input topic of the streaming API. The structure of the input and output topic is identical, allowing multiple iterations of this resource using different parameters without further intervention. The same structure is used in the APIs contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream, contracts-kpi-period-stream, and contracts-kpi-time-series-stream, so that these three APIs can be executed in arbitrary order. After each run, the output topic is enriched with the results of the respective calculation.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the type and level of detail of information in the logs. Logs can be used to analyze problems or processes that occur. The following information can be recorded:
  • ERROR: System malfunctions with slight restrictions for (individual) users are captured.
  • WARN: System malfunctions without restrictions for users are captured.
  • INFO: System state and system activity are captured.
  • DEBUG: System state and system activity as well as additional details are captured.
  • TRACE: Detailed system activity is collected to track typical problems. In addition, detailed information on calculations is provided.

requestBody.description:
The input structure contains the following values:
  • requestId: Freely selectable attribute which can be used to track the request status via the corresponding GET resource. If no value is specified, a UUID is generated. The requestId must be unique.
  • workflowId: Optional attribute for tracking workflows across several asynchronous resources. This ID is added as a log tag and mostly meant to facilitate log analysis. If no value is specified, a default workflow ID may set by the streaming nodes, if such a mechanism is implemented.
  • inputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic from which to read the input messages. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • inputKey: The Kafka message key that identifies the message batch to be processed. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • outputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing results are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing results to Kafka (i.e., if the streaming node is not a data sink).
  • errorTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing errors are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing errors to Kafka (which is strongly recommended for all streaming nodes).
  • cacheSystemVersions: The versions of all relevant cache systems to be used while processing the input messages. Relevant only if the node's business logic relies on reading data from the cache layer. If not set, the node will use the most current cache system versions available. Note, however, that in this case different nodes in a workflow may operate on different data sets, if new cache system versions become available while the workflow is in progress.
  • preferenceContext: The preference context to use when reading configuration values that have been stored as preferences. Relevant only if the node's business logic does that, and if different preference contexts are in use.
  • timeOutInMinutes: Maximum waiting time in minutes, after which still ongoing calculations at the current node are stopped. Can be disabled by setting the value to zero.

In particular, the following values are part of the request section:

  • resultMapKey: Freely selectable attribute with which the results can be referenced. Especially if the same streaming node is passed several times, the individual results can be mapped to their respective parameter selection using the resultMapKey. A functional, descriptive name can be chosen for this identifier.
  • keyFigures: Selection of key figures to be determined. Multi-selection is possible.
    • AVERAGE_VALUE: Average amount of fixed capital. The fixed capital is the average capital invested, based on the specified time period, with payments being considered to the day.
    • TOTAL_RETURN: Gain or loss due to the change in the market value of the holding.
    • RELATIVE_RETURN: Gain/loss proportion measured in terms of the purchase price. If the initial values are zero, the relative gain/loss is displayed as "NaN".
      NOTE: The relative gain or loss does not correspond to the performance. The calculation basis is different.
    • MONEY_WEIGHTED_PERFORMANCE: Growth rate that includes the time and amount of all cash flows in the calculation. It is frequently used to evaluate the performance of a portfolio manager or an active investor.
    • TIME_WEIGHTED_PERFORMANCE: Growth rate of an investment within the valuation period. The investment is evaluated on a daily basis, whilst taking into account the amount of cash flows that have incurred, but not their timing. In practice, this is known as an objective return of an investment over a defined period of time.
    • MODIFIED_DIETZ_PERFORMANCE: Growth rate that reduces the complexity of calculating the money-weighted return. This method has proved itself in practice. It, too, illustrates the performance of the portfolio manager.
    • VOLATILITY: Measure of the daily price fluctuations of a security over a specific time period. A higher volatility means that the prices of a security fluctuate rapidly in a short time span, a lower volatility means that a security's value does not fluctuate dramatically. The higher the standard deviation of a security at a certain point in time, the greater the risk of price losses and the greater the chance of price gains.
    • ANNUAL_VOLATILITY: Annualized version of the volatility, i.e. a measure of the price fluctuations over the course of a year.
    • DOWNSIDE_VOLATILITY: Only focuses on the volatility of negative returns, i.e., the risk of loss. The higher the downside deviation of a security at a certain point in time, the greater the risk of price losses. Price gains are not included in this calculation.
    • ANNUAL_DOWNSIDE_VOLATILITY: Annualized version of the downside volatility.
    • SHARPE_RATIO: Key figure for evaluating the investment success. It shows the ratio of excess return to the risk by comparing the excess return of a financial investment to the standard deviation. The benchmark for risk is the risk-free interest rate. For values greater than 0, the investment under consideration is to be classified as efficient. For values greater than 1, an additional risk is beneficial for the investor, as there is a significant excess return compared to the risk-free investment. Values less than 0 generate lower return than a risk-free investment and are therefore less beneficial to the investor.
    • SORTINO_RATIO: Variation of the Sharpe ratio that does not consider the total volatility of an investment but only factors in the downside volatility. For values greater than 0, the considered investment is efficient. For values greater than 1, an additional risk is beneficial for the investor, as there is a significant additional return compared to the risk-free investment. For values less than 0, lower return is generated than on a risk-free investment and is therefore less beneficial for the investor.
    • VALUE_AT_RISK: Measure of the drop in performance that with a certain probability will not be exceeded within a specified time horizon (e.g., a trading day). Conversely, this means that a loss greater than the reported Value at Risk only occurs with the corresponding counter-probability (which is very small). A Value at Risk (VaR) (95%, 10 days) of 3% means that a maximum loss of 3% will occur within the next 10 days with a probability of 95%, based on the historical development (which is used as a basis). There is a 5% chance that the loss will be greater. The VaR is - in our case - always positive and only considers the probability of losses occurring. The calculated values of the VaR, which indicate the success, are not output.
    • CONDITIONAL_VALUE_AT_RISK: Expected drop in performance if the VaR is actually exceeded. While the Value at Risk (VaR) excludes the worst-case scenarios, the Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) focuses on them: the CVaR represents the mean value of losses that are higher than the VaR. A CVaR (95%, 10 days) of 7% means that within 10 days there is an average maximum loss of 7% in 5% of the worst cases. The CVaR is also—in our case—always positive and only considers the probability of losses occurring. The calculated values of the VaR, which indicate the success, are not output.
    • MAXIMUM_DRAWDOWN: A drawdown is the relative loss between a maximum and the following minimum within a given period. The maximum drawdown is therefore the maximum loss that would occur within a period if the investor had invested at the peak and realized the loss at the subsequent trough of the investment.
  • begin: Begin of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.
  • end: End of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days from 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.
  • aggregations:The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

    Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

    • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
    • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
    • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
    • POSITION: Position
    • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
    • ASSET: Asset
    • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
    • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
    • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
    • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
    • ACCOUNT: Account
    • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
    • MODULE: Module
    • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
    • CONTRACT: Portfolio
    • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)
  • propertyAggregations: The types of asset properties which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • esgValueAggregation: The types of ESG values which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedModules: The module(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculations. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedModules: The module(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssets: The asset(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssets: The asset(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • currencySelection: The logic to use when selecting a currency for each holding. By default, the base currency is used. The base currency is also used if the chosen logic doesn't lead to a unique currency for a given holding.
    • CONTRACT: The currency of the holding's contract.
    • ASSET: The currency of the holding's asset.
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: The currency of the holding's first transaction.
  • currency: The fixed currency to use for each holding. Ignored if the currencySelection parameter is set as well. By default, the base currency is used.
  • valueCalculation: The method to use for calculating transaction values. By default, ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED is used.
    • ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the actual amount.
    • PRICE_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the quantity and quote.
  • paymentCalculation: What kind(s) of payments to include in transaction values. By default, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.
    • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
    • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
    • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
    • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
  • restriction: How to restrict the selection of holdings, if at all. By default, NONE is used.
    • NONE: No restrictions.
    • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Only the holdings that exist at the start of the calculation interval are considered.
    • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Only the holdings that exist at the end of the calculation interval are considered.
  • calculationInterval: How to restrict the calculation interval if necessary. By default, WINDOW_LENGTH is used.
    • WINDOW_LENGTH: The calculation interval is used unchanged.
    • HOLDING_PERIOD: If necessary, the calculation interval is shrunk to the first and last date on which the holding has a non-zero quantity. If there are times of zero quantity within this restricted interval, they are left unchanged.
    • NON_ZERO_HOLDING_PERIOD: The calculation interval is shrunk, and possibly segmented, so that it only includes days on which the holding has a non-zero quantity.
  • includePendingOrders: Whether or not to include pending orders in the calculations. By default, pending orders are not included.
  • ewmaLambda: This parameter determines the lambda value to use for EWMA volatility and value-at-risk calculations. The EWMA calculation is used for values of {@code 0 <= lambda < 1}. For a value of 1.0 the non-EWMA calculation is used. Note that for lambda -> 1 both calculations would yield the same result. If ewmaLambda is not specified, a value will be retrieved from the preferences using the key EWMA_LAMBDA; if that key is not set, a default value of 1.0 will be used and hence the non-EWMA calculation is used by default. by default.

get.summary: Period KPI streaming request status call
get.description:
The GET contracts-kpi-period-stream API determines the state of a specific request initiated by the POST API of the same name using a unique request ID. The following states exist:
  • WAITING: The request with the corresponding ID was initiated but is waiting to be processed.
  • PROCESSING: The request with the corresponding ID is currently being processed.
  • DONE: The request with the corresponding ID is finished. This is a final state.
  • FAILED: The request could not be processed. This is a final state.

NOTE: The outputs numberOfCompletedSteps and numberOfErrors are not supported anymore and thus always zero.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract kpi period calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Time series KPI streaming request status call
post.description:
The POST contracts-kpi-time-series-stream API triggers the calculation of time series of daily holding values, prices, accrued interest as well as daily and total relative returns for a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period. If several key figures are selected for the calculation, the time series are output separately for each key figure.

In particular, this streaming API enables the processing of large numbers of portfolios. The values and key figures to be determined are based on the selection of parameters. It is also possible to show these values or key figures both for the selected holdings separately (e.g., separately for different portfolios) and as an aggregated view of all holdings.

The list of portfolios for which the specified values are to be calculated is determined via the input topic of the streaming API. The structure of the input and output topic is identical, allowing multiple iterations of this resource using different parameters without further intervention. The same structure is used in the APIs contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream, contracts-kpi-period-stream, and contracts-kpi-time-series-stream, so that these three APIs can be executed in arbitrary order. After each run, the output topic is enriched with the results of the respective calculation.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the type and level of detail of information in the logs. Logs can be used to analyze problems or processes that occur. The following information can be recorded:
  • ERROR: System malfunctions with slight restrictions for (individual) users are captured.
  • WARN: System malfunctions without restrictions for users are captured.
  • INFO: System state and system activity are captured.
  • DEBUG: System state and system activity as well as additional details are captured.
  • TRACE: Detailed system activity is collected to track typical problems. In addition, detailed information on calculations is provided.

requestBody.description:
The input structure contains the following values:
  • requestId: Freely selectable attribute which can be used to track the request status via the corresponding GET resource. If no value is specified, a UUID is generated. The requestId must be unique.
  • workflowId: Optional attribute for tracking workflows across several asynchronous resources. This ID is added as a log tag and mostly meant to facilitate log analysis. If no value is specified, a default workflow ID may set by the streaming nodes, if such a mechanism is implemented.
  • inputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic from which to read the input messages. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • inputKey: The Kafka message key that identifies the message batch to be processed. Mandatory if the node is set up to read input messages from Kafka (i.e., if the node is not a data source and input messages are not included in the request itself).
  • outputTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing results are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing results to Kafka (i.e., if the streaming node is not a data sink).
  • errorTopic: The name of the Kafka topic to which the processing errors are written. Mandatory if the node is set up to write processing errors to Kafka (which is strongly recommended for all streaming nodes).
  • cacheSystemVersions: The versions of all relevant cache systems to be used while processing the input messages. Relevant only if the node's business logic relies on reading data from the cache layer. If not set, the node will use the most current cache system versions available. Note, however, that in this case different nodes in a workflow may operate on different data sets, if new cache system versions become available while the workflow is in progress.
  • preferenceContext: The preference context to use when reading configuration values that have been stored as preferences. Relevant only if the node's business logic does that, and if different preference contexts are in use.
  • timeOutInMinutes: Maximum waiting time in minutes, after which still ongoing calculations at the current node are stopped. Can be disabled by setting the value to zero.

In particular, the following values are part of the request section:

  • resultMapKey: Freely selectable attribute with which the results can be referenced. Especially if the same streaming node is passed several times, the individual results can be mapped to their respective parameter selection using the resultMapKey. A functional, descriptive name can be chosen for this identifier.
  • keyFigures: Selection of key figures to be determined. There may be dependencies between the chosen key figure(s) and the parameter aggregation. Some key figures can only be determined if the chosen aggregation methods include ASSET and/or POSITION. Multi-selection is possible.
    • QUANTITY_CORRECTION: Daily correction values to adjust nominal values of bonds for calculation. Quantity corrections are solely applicable for pool factor bonds and inflation-linked bonds. This requires aggregation by ASSET and/or POSITION.
    • QUOTE: Daily quotes of security and liquidity holdings. This requires aggregation by ASSET and/or POSITION.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST_RATE: Fraction of the nominal which the customer has as accrued interest claim on the respective day. This accrued interest rate is independent of the actual nominals held. This requires aggregation by ASSET and/or POSITION.
    • QUANTITY: Daily number of asset units.
    • VALUE: Daily security and liquidity holdings values.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST: Accrued interest which the customer has as claim on the respective day. This is the monetary value the customer were to gain as accrued interest, if they sold their bonds on the respective day.
    • DAILY_YIELD: Relative gain or loss compared to the previous day. A value of 1 resembles no gain or loss, higher than 1 a gain, and lower than 1 a loss, compared to the previous day.
    • TOTAL_YIELD: Product of all daily yields since the start of the requested period up to the respective day. NOTE: The total yield does not correspond to the relative return. The calculation basis is different.
  • begin: Begin of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.
  • end: End of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days from 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.
  • aggregations:The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

    Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

    • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
    • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
    • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
    • POSITION: Position
    • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
    • ASSET: Asset
    • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
    • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
    • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
    • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
    • ACCOUNT: Account
    • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
    • MODULE: Module
    • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
    • CONTRACT: Portfolio
    • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)
  • propertyAggregations: The types of asset properties which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • esgValueAggregation: The categories of ESG values which the results are aggregated by. These can be specified as an alternative to the aggregation methods mentioned above, or in addition to them. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedModules: The module(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculations. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedModules: The module(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssets: The asset(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssets: The asset(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • selectedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be considered exclusively in the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • excludedAssetTypes: The asset type(s) that should be excluded from the calculation. Multi-selection is possible.
  • currencySelection: The logic to use when selecting a currency for each holding. By default, the base currency is used. The base currency is also used if the chosen logic doesn't lead to a unique currency for a given holding.
    • CONTRACT: The currency of the holding's contract.
    • ASSET: The currency of the holding's asset.
    • OPENING_TRANSACTION: The currency of the holding's first transaction.
  • currency: The fixed currency to use for each holding. Ignored if the currencySelection parameter is set as well. By default, the base currency is used.
  • valueCalculation: The method to use for calculating transaction values. By default, ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED is used.
    • ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the actual amount.
    • PRICE_BASED: Calculate transaction values based on the quantity and quote.
  • paymentCalculation: What kind(s) of payments to include in transaction values. By default, ALL_PAYMENTS is used.
    • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
    • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
    • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
    • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
    • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
    • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
  • restriction: How to restrict the selection of holdings, if at all. By default, NONE is used.
    • NONE: No restrictions.
    • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Only the holdings that exist at the start of the calculation interval are considered.
    • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Only the holdings that exist at the end of the calculation interval are considered.
  • calculationInterval: How to restrict the calculation interval if necessary. By default, WINDOW_LENGTH is used.
    • WINDOW_LENGTH: The calculation interval is used unchanged.
    • HOLDING_PERIOD: If necessary, the calculation interval is shrunk to the first and last date on which the holding has a non-zero quantity. If there are times of zero quantity within this restricted interval, they are left unchanged.
    • NON_ZERO_HOLDING_PERIOD: The calculation interval is shrunk, and possibly segmented, so that it only includes days on which the holding has a non-zero quantity.
  • scaleValue: The value to which the first entry of all time series within the calculation interval should be set. All other values of each time series are then scaled accordingly.
    This can be useful to ensure the comparability of time series. E.g., if you want all total yield series to start at 100%, you can achieve that by specifying a scale value of 1.0.
    Note that the scaling effectively destroys the original information for the first entry in each time series. E.g., if you scale a total yield series you effectively lose the performance of the first day in the result. You can avoid this by starting the calculation interval a day early, e.g. if you want to display a time series starting with 2022-01-01, you can start the calculation interval at 2021-12-31 and then discard the first entry of the time series when displaying it.
    Also note that while scaling will always be performed if you request it, it doesn't necessarily make sense for all the time series you can query with this resource. It's included primarily as a feature for the total yield series. It may also be useful to make other time series comparable to each other, but it probably doesn't make much sense e.g. for a quantity series.
  • includePendingOrders: Whether or not to include pending orders in the calculations. By default, pending orders are not included.

get.summary: Time series KPI streaming request status call
get.description:
The GET contracts-kpi-time-series-stream API determines the state of a specific request initiated by the POST API of the same name using a unique request ID. The following states exist:
  • WAITING: The request with the corresponding ID was initiated but is waiting to be processed.
  • PROCESSING: The request with the corresponding ID is currently being processed.
  • DONE: The request with the corresponding ID is finished. This is a final state.
  • FAILED: The request could not be processed. This is a final state.

NOTE: The outputs numberOfCompletedSteps and numberOfErrors are not supported anymore and thus always zero.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract kpi time series calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Registers an instance of the streaming API node with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations.
post.description:
This resource provides entites to use in a streaming workflow. Selection developed flexibly and needs to be registered as a plugin. Additionally filter can be developed, registered and referenced in the request.

The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the entity selection process
get.description:
Each request for the entity selection calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the selection process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the entity selection was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the entity selection is currently being processed

DONE—the entity selection is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the selection, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the entity selection request.

404.description: Not Found

post.summary: Triggers a conversion of non-standard data to standardized streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a conversion of some kind of non-standard data, which is expected to be provided as input messages via Kafka. Depending on the given conversion source, the messages are converted to one type of standardized streaming data messages, such as contract streaming data messages, which are then provided as output messages via Kafka. The input messages themselves are included in the streaming data messages' result map, using the given result map key and the sub-key originalMessage.

The purpose of the GET resource is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of an entity conversion process
get.description:
Each request for the entity-conversion-stream resource has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the conversion process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the conversion was initiated but is waiting to be processed
