PROCESSING—the conversion is currently being processed

DONE—the conversion is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of entities that have been processed, and how many of them were converted successfully or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the entity conversion request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Triggers the creation of a contract set from contract streaming data
post.description:
This resource triggers the creation of a contract set from contract streaming data messages that must be provided in the given input topic. A unique contract set ID must be specified in the request for this resource. A new contract set with this ID is then created asynchronously, and every contract that appears in an input message is added to this contract set. Afterwards, the contract set can can be used with any other Analytics resource that supports contract sets.

The contract set is created as mutable, meaning it can be changed later by adding or removing contracts via the /portfolio/contract-sets resources. Other properties of the contract set, such as its lifetime, can be specified in the request for this resource. The contract set is assigned to the user calling this resource.

The GET resource associated with this resource can be used to query the status of the asynchronous computation. The contract set can be used once the status becomes DONE.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

get.summary: State of a contract set creation process
get.description:
Each contract set creation request has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the selection process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the entity selection was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the entity selection is currently being processed

DONE—the entity selection is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of contracts that have been processed so far, and how many of these contracts were processed successfully, or failed because of an error.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract set creation request.

404.description: Not Found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations.
The node will add a list of transactions and/or transaction statistics to the contract streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received from a Kafka topic, and decorates these contracts with a list of transactions and/or transaction statistics for the given time interval.

If transactions are selected, they are added to the result with a key contractsTransactions, using the same result structure as the /portfolio/transactions end point of the synchronous REST API.

If transaction statistics are selected, they are added to the result with a key contractsTransactionStatistics, using the same result structure as the /portfolio/transaction-statistics end point of the synchronous REST API.

The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing, and if so, with what kind of result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the contract transaction calculation process
get.description:
Each request for the contract transaction calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the transaction process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the process was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the process is currently being processed

DONE—the process is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the transaction calculation, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract transaction calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations. The node adds
comprehensive return information to the contracts streaming data.
post.description:
The average-holding-properties API endpoint generates the average holding properties of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) on a specified date. The calculation of a property's mean value only makes sense for those properties where the characteristics of the property can be expressed in figures. This is the case, for example, for security ratings. Ratings assess the creditworthiness of the respective companies, states or countries that issue a financial product. The result will be added under the key contractsAverageHoldingProperties

The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the contract return calculation process
get.description:
Each request for the contract return calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the return process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the return calculation was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the return calculation is currently being processed

DONE—the contract return calculation is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the return calculation, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract return calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations.
The node adds detailed fee and/or tax information to the contract streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received on a Kafka topic and decorates these contracts with detailed fee and/or tax information. Fees and/or taxes are broken down according to any known detailed type information. The result can be restricted to specific detailed types if so desired, or specific detailed types can be excluded. The result will be added to the contract streaming data under the key contractsCostDetails.

Note that if you only need a summary of total costs, the contracts-returns-stream node may be better suited for your purposes.

The purpose of the GET resources is to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the contract cost details calculation process
get.description:
Each request for the contract cost details calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the cost details process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING — the cost details calculation was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING — the cost details calculation is currently being processed

DONE — the cost details calculation is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the cost details calculation, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract cost details calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations. The resource adds
selected holding data to the contract streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received on a kafka topic and decorates these contracts with selected holding data information. The holding data is added under the selection key of each aspect. Available selections are:
  • CONTRACT: Adds display details for the contract
  • PERSON: Adds display details for persons that are related to the contract
  • ORGANIZATION: Adds display details for organizations that are related to the contract
  • ACCOUNT: Adds display details for the accounts of the contract
  • POSITION: Adds display details for the positions of the contract
  • RESTRICTION: Adds display details for the trading restrictions of the contract

The resource accepts either a when date for a snapshot-oriented view on the holding data or a begin and end date for a period view. The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the process of adding selected holding data.
get.description:
Each request for the holding detail selection has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the holding data process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the process was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the process is currently being processed

DONE—the process is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the process, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contracts holding data stream request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations. The resource adds
selected market data to the contract streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received on a kafka topic and decorates these contracts with selected market data information. This node accepts either a period (begin and end must be set), or a snapshot (only when is set). The market data is added under the selection key of each aspect. If a period (begin-end) is specified the selection shows all relevant master data, market data, properties and, if applicable the underlying structure for assets of all holdings that belong to a specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) over a specified time period. The master data includes the name, ISIN, inception date and the security type. The market data contains the price in the base currency and various interest data for the end of the regarded time period. Furthermore, the proportion of an asset property (e.g., region, country, sector) is calculated. If an asset is a fund, derivative or foreign currency, the underlying financial instruments are output with the inclusion of the asset proportion and a selection of master data.

If only a snapshot (when) is specified the selection will simply show the moment of the last interest payment. The only difference between both selections is thus the parameters that specify the time when the holding context (e.g., a portfolio) is reviewed (begin, end and when). Beyond that, the two selections are identical in use.

The return structure matches the result of the /portfolio/assets-period and /portfolio-assets-snapshot endpoints. The resource accepts either a when date for a snapshot-oriented view on the holding data or a begin and end date for a period view. The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the process of adding selected market data.
get.description:
Each request for the market detail selection has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the market data process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the process was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the process is currently being processed

DONE—the process is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the process, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contracts market data stream request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary:
Orchestrates the streaming API with the relevant topics and provides parameters for the calculations. The node adds
comprehensive return information to the contracts streaming data.
post.description:
This resource triggers a processing of contracts received on a kafka topic and decorates these contracts with return information. For a total return value, use the contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream. This node offers more detailed return information, listing realized, unrealized, return taxes, etc. The result will be added under the key contractsReturns

The purpose of the GET resources is then to show if the request has finished processing and with which result.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom Log level

get.summary: State of the contract return calculation process
get.description:
Each request for the contract return calculation has a unique ID. This request ID can be entered here to get the state of the return process.

The system differentiates between the following states:

WAITING—the return calculation was initiated but is waiting to be processed

PROCESSING—the return calculation is currently being processed

DONE—the contract return calculation is completed

Additionally, this resource shows the total number of steps taken during the return calculation, and how many of these steps were completed or failed because an error occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the contract return calculation request.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

post.summary: Triggers the creation of temporary contracts based on contract streaming data
post.description:
This resource triggers the creation of temporary contracts based on contract streaming data messages that must be provided in the given input topic. For each contract, the resource determines the contract's holdings on a given sourceDate, and creates a temporary contract with the same holdings on a given investment date (typically one that lies in the past relative to the source date). The temporary contracts can then be used for further analysis (whether by other streaming nodes or by calls to synchronous endpoints). In cases where the quote history of an asset is missing or is of poor quality, the optional parameters invalidQuoteHandling and useProxyAssets can be used to improve the robustness of the simulation.

Optionally, the resource can be told to replace the original contract IDs with the associated temporary contract IDs in the outgoing contract streaming data messages, so that downstream nodes will automatically work on the temporary contracts rather than the original contracts. In this case, the resource can also be told to preserve the original contract IDs in the parameters map of the messages, with a key specified in the request for this resource.

Also optionally, the resource can be told to generate temporary contract IDs that are based on the original contract IDs, rather than using UUIDs. In this case, the temporary contracts will receive IDs in the format <prefix>:<originalContractId>, where the prefix can also be specified. This allows callers of other endpoints to access a temporary contract directly as long as they know the original contract ID, since the temporary contract ID can be constructed from that.

Note that, in contrast to most other streaming nodes that work on contract streaming data messages, this resource does not add anything to the messages' result maps.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

post.summary: Triggers the deletion of temporary contracts from contract streaming data
post.description:
This resource triggers the deletion of temporary contracts based on contract streaming data messages that must be provided in the given input topic. For each contract, the resource tries to interpret it as a temporary contract, and delete its data from the database. If a contract can't be deleted as a temporary contract for any reason (e.g. because it's not a temporary contract, or because it was never persisted in the database, or because it was already deleted), nothing happens for that contract.

Calling this resource is essentially an optimization step to ensure that temporary contracts which are no longer needed for a workflow are not kept around unnecessarily. It has no effect on the business logic of a workflow, and it's never necessary in the sense that temporary contracts will be deleted anyway once they've reached their expiration time. However, given that the streaming API is typically used for mass operations involving large numbers of messages, it can still be a good idea to include this resource in workflows that use temporary contracts.

Note that, in contrast to most other streaming nodes that work on contract streaming data messages, this resource does not add anything to the messages' result maps.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

post.summary: Streaming Data Persistence Streaming Request
post.description: The POST contract-streaming-data-persistence-stream API triggers the persistence of data from the ContractStreamingData object to the database. It is possible, to configure, which parts of the streaming data should be persisted. The structure and the content of the input and output topic is identical, so that this endpoint can be added to a workflow without further impact on the data. The same structure is used in the APIs contracts-kpi-snapshot-stream, contracts-kpi-period-stream, and contracts-kpi-time-series-stream, so that this endpoint can be added after several other APIs to persist the data gathered in other runs.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description:
This parameter determines the type and level of detail of information in the logs. Logs can be used to analyze problems or processes that occur. The following information can be recorded:
  • ERROR: System malfunctions with slight restrictions for (individual) users are captured.
  • WARN: System malfunctions without restrictions for users are captured.
  • INFO: System state and system activity are captured.
  • DEBUG: System state and system activity as well as additional details are captured.
  • TRACE: Detailed system activity is collected to track typical problems. In addition, detailed information on calculations is provided.

requestBody.description:
Besides the standard fields for an asynchronous computation, there are the following request parameters:
  • persistenceKey: the key used to persist (and later find) the data in the database, this may be for example a workflowId or a reasonId
  • persistenceData: the configuration, which data should be persisted, consisting of an array of objects with the following fields:
    • resultMapKey: the key of the result data in ContractStreamingData to be persisted
    • holdingResultMapKeys: an array of keys of the result data in the inner HoldingResultEntry to be persisted

get.description:
Calling POST contract-streaming-data-persistence-stream API will persist a selected subset of ContractStreamingData which can be queried by GET report/contract-streaming-data/{persistenceKey}.

The persistenceKey is a parameter of the aforementioned streaming API endpoint and may be a workflowId or a reasonId.

parameters.name: persistenceKey
parameters.description: The unique ID of the persisted set of ContractStreamingData.

404.description: Unknown request ID or no results found

get.summary: Shows the definitions of supported entities
get.description: This resource shows the definitions of all entities that are supported by the other data-provisioning resources, including mandatory fields, possible search indexes, etc.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

get.description:
This resource retrieves a record for the specified entity with the given ID values.

The given ID values must match the ID index for the specified entity. To find out which fields make up the ID index for a given entity, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be queried in this way.

Note that in contrast to most other analytics resources, this one reads data from the database rather than the cache layer, so the results may not always agree with the results of other resources.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity from which to read

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: An ID field

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: An ID field value

put.summary: Creates or updates a database record
put.description:
This resource creates or updates a database record for the specified entity with the given field values.

The given field values must match the data model for the specified entity. To query this data model, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be written to in this way. To create a new record, field values must include at least all mandatory fields. To update an existing record, field values must include at least all ID fields.

If no record with the same ID values as the given ones exists for the specified entity, one is created, otherwise the existing one is updated. Alternatively, if the given field values include a record state of "DELETED", the existing record will be logically deleted.

Note that this resource only updates the database, not the cache layer, so any changes may not become visible in other analytics resources for some time (although they will become visible in other data-provisioning resources immediately). To ensure that they become visible in other resources, you may have to trigger the creation of a new cache version yourself.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity to which to write

requestBody.description: A JSON object containing the fields and values to write

delete.description:
This resource deletes a record from the specified entity with the given ID values.

The given ID values must match the ID index for the specified entity. To find out which fields make up the ID index for a given entity, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be deleted from in this way.

Note that this resource only updates the database, not the cache layer, so any changes may not become visible in other analytics resources for some time (although they will become visible in other data-provisioning resources immediately). To ensure that they become visible in other resources, you may have to trigger the creation of a new cache version yourself.

Also note that this resource performs a physical delete, i.e. it removes the affected record entirely from the database. This means that the change may require the creation of a complete cache version, rather than an incremental one, before it becomes visible in the cache layer. Generally it is recommended to start by deleting a database record logically, by updating it with record state DELETED, and then delete it physically some time later (if at all).

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity from which to delete

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: An ID field

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: An ID field value

get.summary: Retrieves one or more database records
get.description:
This resource retrieves one or more records for the specified entity that match the given search key.

The given search key must match one of the supported search indexes for the specified entity. To find out which search indexes are supported, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be queried in this way.

Note that in contrast to most other analytics resources, this one reads data from the database rather than the cache layer, so the results may not always agree with the results of other resources.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity from which to read

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: A search key field

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: A search key field value

put.summary: Creates or updates one or more database records
put.description:
This resource creates or updates one or more database records for the specified entity with the given field values.

The given field values must match the data model for the specified entity. To query this data model, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be written to in this way. To create a new record, field values must include at least all mandatory fields. To update an existing record, field values must include at least all ID fields.

If a given record doesn't match the ID values of an existing record for the specified entity, a new record is created, otherwise the existing one is updated. Alternatively, if the given field values include a record state of "DELETED", the existing record will be logically deleted.

Multiple records are always handled in the given order, which can be useful if records depend on each other. E.g. if you want to create two new records, and one of them references the other, you can create both of them with a single call by ordering them so that the referenced record appears first in the list.

Note that this resource only updates the database, not the cache layer, so any changes may not become visible in other analytics resources for some time (although they will become visible in other data-provisioning resources immediately). To ensure that they become visible in other resources, you may have to trigger the creation of a new cache version yourself.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity to which to write

requestBody.description: A JSON array, with each object in the array containing the fields and values of one record to write

This resource deletes one or more records from the specified entity that match the given search key.

The given search key must match one of the supported search indexes for the specified entity. To find out which search indexes are supported, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be deleted from in this way.

Note that this resource only updates the database, not the cache layer, so any changes may not become visible in other analytics resources for some time (although they will become visible in other data-provisioning resources immediately). To ensure that they become visible in other resources, you may have to trigger the creation of a new cache version yourself.

Also note that this resource performs a physical delete, i.e. it removes the affected record entirely from the database. This means that the change may require the creation of a complete cache version, rather than an incremental one, before it becomes visible in the cache layer. Generally it is recommended to start by deleting a database record logically, by updating it with record state DELETED, and then delete it physically some time later (if at all).

This resource creates or updates one or more database records for one or more entities with the given field values.

The given field values must match the data model for the corresponding entity. To query this data model, see the "entity-definitions" resource. The same resource also provides information about which entities can be written to in this way. To create a new record, field values must include at least all mandatory fields. To update an existing record, field values must include at least all ID fields.

If a given record doesn't match the ID values of an existing record for the specified entity, a new record is created, otherwise the existing one is updated. Alternatively, if the given field values include a record state of "DELETED", the existing record will be logically deleted.

Multiple records are always handled in the given order, which can be useful if records depend on each other. E.g. if you want to create two new records, and one of them references the other, you can create both of them with a single call by ordering them so that the referenced record appears first in the list.

Note that this resource only updates the database, not the cache layer, so any changes may not become visible in other analytics resources for some time (although they will become visible in other data-provisioning resources immediately). To ensure that they become visible in other resources, you may have to trigger the creation of a new cache version yourself.

parameters.name: X-Log-Level
parameters.description: Custom log level

requestBody.description:
A JSON array, with each object in the array containing one entity to write to,
and the fields and values of one record to write

get.summary: API error information for the given error type

resultMapKey.description: This parameter specifies the name under which the result will be added to the result map.

when.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

aggregation.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

propertyAggregation.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties. The selection of such properties can be configured.

esgValueAggregation.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

selectedModule.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedModule.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

selectedAsset.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedAsset.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

selectedAssetType.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedAssetType.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

currencySelection.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

currency.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

quoteCurrencySelection.description:
This parameter specifies the quote currency in which results for quote, exchange rate, initial price, and initial currency are shown.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.

If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the quote currency is the same as the currency in which the other results are calculated.

quoteCurrency.description:
This parameter determines the quote currency in which calculation results for quote, exchange rate, initial price, and initial currency are shown.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter quouteCurrencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both quoteCurrencySelection and quoteCurrency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter quoteCurrencySelection. If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the quote currency is the same as the currency in which the other results are calculated.

exchangeRateSelection.description:
This parameter specifies the exchange rate selection in which comparative results for exchange rate and initial currency price are shown.

The following values are available:

  • QUOTE_CURRENCY_VS_BASE_CURRENCY: Shows the result in Quote currency vs Base currency.
  • QUOTE_CURRENCY_VS_HOLDING_CURRENCY: Shows the result in comparison of Quote Currency vs Holding Currency.

If no quote currency is selected via one of the quoteCurrencySelection or quoteCurrency parameters, the exchange rate and initial currency price will be in the same currency in which the other results are calculated.

additionalKeyFigure.description:
This parameter enables the inclusion of the following additional key figures in the output:
  • VALUATION: Key figures to evaluate portfolios. Selecting this value will add information on inconsistent holdings. Inconsistent holdings occur when the holdings information of aixigo's system does not correspond to the holdings information of the core banking system.
    NOTE:There is a dependency between this parameter value and the parameter aggregation. VALUATION can only be selected in combination with the values ASSET and/or POSITION of the aggregation parameter. If the aggregation method is ASSET, existing valuations that are position-based are aggregated by asset accordingly. However, if the aggregation method is POSITION, asset-based valuations are not subdivided but instead treated as if they do not exist.

  • INVESTMENTS: Adds information on transactions that resulted in the respective holding. This information includes the transaction type, date of the transaction (i.e., the day on which the security was actually traded), quantities and value of the financial product purchased or sold. In addition, the data quality of the data that enables this information to be provided is shown.

  • RETURN_SINCE_BEGIN: Adds the following results to the output:
    Realized and unrealized
    - absolute price gain/loss (totalReturn)
    - accrued interest (accruedInterest)
    - price-based gain/loss (priceBasedReturn)
    - currency-based gain/loss (currencyBasedReturn)
    - credits (creditAdvices)
    - fees (fees)
    - taxes (taxes)
    The start of the period under consideration is the date of the first holding. Its end is the reference date specified under parameter when.

  • PERFORMANCE_SINCE_BEGIN: Calculates the Modified Dietz performance (based on weighted cash flows), money-weighted performance (based on the internal rate of return) and time-weighted performance (based on daily returns). The start of the period under consideration is the date of the first holding. Its end is the reference date specified under parameter when.
Multi-selection is possible. The default value is "-" (no selection).

additionalAssetData.description:
This parameter enables the inclusion of additional asset data in the output.

The following values are available:

  • BASIC_DATA: Shows basic asset data, such as ISIN, local ID, notation type, issue date, maturity date, etc., for each holding.

  • RELATIONSHIPS: Shows asset-to-asset relationships, such as underlyings or asset compositions, for each holding.

  • PROPERTIES: Shows all properties and property proportions that were available on the specified date for each holding.

  • QUOTE: Shows quotes and exchange rates that were available on the specified date for each holding.

  • INTEREST: Shows the interest rate on the specified date and the previously valid interest rate for each holding.
    NOTE:There is a dependency between the parameters additionalAssetData and aggregation. If one of the above-mentioned options was selected for additionalAssetData, the parameter aggregation must be filled with ASSET and/or POSITION.

  • DERIVATIVE: Shows derivatives with all risks that were available on the specified date for each holding.

Multi-selection is possible. The default value is "-" (no selection).

includeTotals.description:
This parameter determines whether a total overview of the portfolio should be given in the output. If, for example, the aggregation method is POSITION because the output values are to be specified for each security position, specifying this parameter also adds the output values for the selected holding context, e.g., a specific portfolio. If several portfolios were selected for analysis, the algorithm calculates the output values for the so-called consolidated portfolio. A consolidated portfolio is, for example, the combination of two portfolios into one overall portfolio.

NOTE: There may be dependencies between the parameter includeTotals and other parameters. If, for example, the currency is selected with inconsistent parameter settings, all results are shown in the base currency. This can limit the comparability of the output results.

The following values are available:

  • true: Outputs the consolidated view of the portfolio.
  • false: Disregards consolidation.
The default value is false (disregard consolidated view).

includePendingOrders.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

assessmentType.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

resultMapKey.description: This parameter specifies the name under which the result will be added to the result map.

keyFigures.description:
This parameter specifies the key figure or figures that should be calculated for each holding and added to the result map.

The following values are available:

  • QUANTITY: The holding's quantity on the given date.
  • VALUE: The holding's value on the given date.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST: The holding's accrued interest on the given date.
  • INITIAL_COST: The initial cost of the holding's contents on the given date.
  • INITIAL_PRICE: The initial price of the holding's contents on the given date.
  • INITIAL_CURRENCY_PRICE: The initial currency price of the holding's contents on the given date.
  • VALUE_CHANGE: The difference between the holding's value on the given date and its initial cost.
  • RELATIVE_VALUE_CHANGE: The difference between the holding's value on the given date and its initial cost, relative to this initial cost.
  • VALUATION_GAP: The holding's valuation gap on the given date, if any. Note: Be aware that the calculation of valuation gaps is not compatible with the inclusion of pending orders, since the inclusion of pending orders leads to virtual transactions that do not have valuation counterparts. If you set the parameter includePendingOrders to true, all valuation gaps will be reported as null.
  • DAILY_VOLATILITY: The holding's volatility on the given date.
  • ANNUAL_VOLATILITY: The holding's annualized volatility on the given date.
  • CONTRIBUTION_TO_RISK: The holding's contribution to the contract's overall risk.
  • WEIGHTED_CONTRIBUTION_TO_RISK;colon; The holding's weighted contribution to the contract's overall risk.

when.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the key figures should be calculated. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

aggregations.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)
Note that for this resource, CONTRACT is always implicitly chosen, but any number of additional methods can be specified.

propertyAggregations.description: This parameter specifies one or more asset property types by which the holdings to analyze are aggregated (in addition to any aggregation methods specified by the aggregations parameter).

esgValueAggregation.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

selectedModules.description: This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

excludedModules.description: This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

selectedAssets.description: This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

excludedAssets.description: This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

selectedAssetTypes.description: This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

excludedAssetTypes.description: This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

currencySelection.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected, the calculation is made in the fixed currency specified by the currency parameter, or in the base currency if that parameter isn't set either.

currency.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected if the currencySelection parameter is not set. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the currency parameter is ignored, and only the currencySelection parameter is used.

valueCalculation.description:
This parameter determines the calculation method for the value of a transaction.

The following values are available:

  • ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED: Determines the value of a transaction based on the data passed by the parameter actualAmount defined in the data model. It is the legal value of the transaction that is delivered from the financial institution's core banking system.
  • PRICE_BASED: Determines the value of a transaction based on the product of the purchased and sold quantities and the security price. Depending on the selection of the parameter paymentCalculation, the value of the transaction also increases by the value of the accrued interest claims and credits received or decreases by the value of the taxes and fees paid.
Ideally, the values of the transactions are the same regardless of whether ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED or PRICE_BASED is selected. However, this might not always be the case due to inaccuracies in the data supply.

The default value is ACTUAL_AMOUNT_BASED.

paymentCalculation.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as accrued interest, credit advices, fees and/or taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
The default value is ALL_PAYMENTS.

includePendingOrders.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

assessmentType.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

resultMapKey.description: This parameter specifies the name under which the result will be added to the result map.

keyFigures.description:
This parameter specifies the key figure or figures that should be calculated for each holding and added to the result map.

The following values are available:

  • AVERAGE_VALUE: The holding's average value during the given time interval.
  • TOTAL_RETURN: The holding's absolute gain or loss during the given time interval.
  • RELATIVE_RETURN: The holding's gain or loss during the given time interval, relative to its value at the beginning of the time interval.
  • MODIFIED_DIETZ_PERFORMANCE: The holding's performance during the time interval, calculated by the Modified Dietz algorithm.
  • MONEY_WEIGHTED_PERFORMANCE: The holding's performance during the time interval, calculated by the money-weighted algorithm.
  • TIME_WEIGHTED_PERFORMANCE: The holding's performance during the time interval, calculated by the time-weighted algorithm.
  • VOLATILITY: The holding's daily volatility during the time interval.
  • ANNUAL_VOLATILITY: The holding's annual volatility during the time interval.
  • DOWNSIDE_VOLATILITY: The holding's daily downside volatility during the time interval.
  • ANNUAL_DOWNSIDE_VOLATILITY: The holding's annual downside volatility during the time interval.
  • SHARPE_RATIO: The holding's Sharpe ratio during the time interval.
  • SORTINO_RATIO: The holding's Sortino ratio during the time interval.
  • VALUE_AT_RISK: The holding's value at risk during the time interval.
  • CONDITIONAL_VALUE_AT_RISK: The holding's conditional value at risk during the time interval.
  • MAXIMUM_DRAWDOWN: The holding's maximum drawdown during the time interval.

begin.description: This parameter specifies the start date (inclusive) of the time interval for which the key figures should be calculated. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

end.description: This parameter specifies the end date (exclusive) of the time interval for which the key figures should be calculated. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

paymentCalculation.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which payments, such as accrued interest, credit advices, fees and/or taxes, are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • NO_PAYMENTS: Disregards all payments.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_ONLY: Includes accrued interest only.
  • CREDIT_ADVICES_ONLY: Includes credit advices only.
  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fees only.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes taxes only.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and credit advices, but disregards fees and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_TAXES: Includes accrued interest and fees, but disregards credit advices and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES_AND_FEES: Includes accrued interest and taxes, but disregards credit advices and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_TAXES: Includes credit advices and fees, but disregards accrued interest and taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_FEES: Includes credit advices and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST_AND_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes fees and taxes, but disregards accrued interest and credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_TAXES: Includes all payments except taxes.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_FEES: Includes all payments except fees.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_CREDIT_ADVICES: Includes all payments except credit advices.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS_EXCEPT_ACCRUED_INTEREST: Includes all payments except accrued interest.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes all payments.
The default value is ALL_PAYMENTS.

restriction.description:
This parameter determines how financial instruments are included in the calculation. The restriction relates to the financial instruments that belong to the specific holding context (e.g., a portfolio) or their properties that have changed in the regarded time period. It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

calculationInterval.description:
This parameter specifies the time period over which holdings are to be considered and included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • WINDOW_LENGTH: Uses the specified time period without adjustments.
  • HOLDING_PERIOD: If necessary, moves the start date of the specified time period forward to the first day on which the holding's quantity is non-zero, and/or moves the end date backward to the day after the last day on which the holding's quantity is non-zero. In other words, times of zero quantity at the beginning and end of the specified time period are left out of the calculation. Times of zero quantity within the restricted time period are still included in the calculation.
  • NON_ZERO_HOLDING_PERIOD: Restricts the specified time period so that only days on which the holding's quantity is non-zero are included in the calculation.
The default value is WINDOW_LENGTH.

includePendingOrders.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

assessmentType.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

resultMapKey.description: This parameter specifies the name under which the result will be added to the result map.

keyFigures.description:
This parameter specifies the key figure or figures that should be calculated for each holding and added to the result map.

The following values are available:

  • QUANTITY: The time series of the holding's quantities during the time interval.
  • QUANTITY_CORRECTION: The time series of the holding's quantity corrections during the time interval.
  • QUOTE: The time series of the holding's quotes during the time interval.
  • VALUE: The time series of the holding's values during the time interval.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST_RATE: The time series of the holding's accrued interest rates during the time interval.
  • ACCRUED_INTEREST: The time series of the holding's accrued interest during the time interval.
  • DAILY_YIELD: The time series of the holding's daily yields during the time interval.
  • TOTAL_YIELD: The time series of the holding's time-weighted performance during the time interval.

begin.description: This parameter specifies the start date (inclusive) of the time interval for which the time series should be calculated. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

end.description: This parameter specifies the end date (exclusive) of the time interval for which the time series should be calculated. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

It can be of particular importance when considering convertible bonds or maturities.
  • Convertible bonds can be converted from a bond to a share over time. Assuming that the security is actually converted into one or more shares on day Z, it must be communicated to the API which type of security the convertible bond is to be regarded as at what time. Selecting NONE maps the reality, i.e., the convertible bond is considered a bond until day Z and as share from that day on. HOLDINGS_AT_START regards the security as a bond for the entire period under consideration, and HOLDINGS_AT_END as a bond for the entire analysis period.
  • Maturities, i.e., the time at which a bond is due, decrease over time. If securities should be analyzed with a maturity date of < 3 years, it must be decided whether the maturity date of these securities is at the beginning or end of the analysis. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_START, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the beginning of the period. By selecting HOLDINGS_AT_END, the system analyzes all securities that have a maturity date of < 3 years at the end of the period.
The following values are available:
  • NONE: Includes the financial instrument in the calculation according to the actual holding changes, i.e., holdings that change over the regarded period are included in the calculation according to the proportion of time they were in the holding.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_START: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the beginning of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the beginning of the regarded period are included.
  • HOLDINGS_AT_END: Includes the financial instrument in the analysis for the entire period according to its holding valuation at the end of the period, i.e., only those holdings that exist at the end of the regarded period are included.

calculationInterval.description:
This parameter specifies the time period over which holdings are to be considered and included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • WINDOW_LENGTH: Uses the specified time period without adjustments.
  • HOLDING_PERIOD: If necessary, moves the start date of the specified time period forward to the first day on which the holding's quantity is non-zero, and/or moves the end date backward to the day after the last day on which the holding's quantity is non-zero. In other words, times of zero quantity at the beginning and end of the specified time period are left out of the calculation. Times of zero quantity within the restricted time period are still included in the calculation.
  • NON_ZERO_HOLDING_PERIOD: Restricts the specified time period so that only days on which the holding's quantity is non-zero are included in the calculation.
The default value is WINDOW_LENGTH.

scaleValue.description:
The value to which the first entry of all time series within the calculation interval should be set. All other values of each time series are then scaled accordingly.

This can be useful to ensure the comparability of time series. E.g., if you want all total yield series to start at 100%, you can achieve that by specifying a scale value of 1.0.

Note that the scaling effectively destroys the original information for the first entry in each time series. E.g., if you scale a total yield series you effectively lose the performance of the first day in the result. You can avoid this by starting the calculation interval a day early, e.g. if you want to display a time series starting with 2022-01-01, you can start the calculation interval at 2021-12-31 and then discard the first entry of the time series when displaying it.

Also note that while scaling will always be performed if you request it, it doesn't necessarily make sense for all the time series you can query with this resource. It's included primarily as a feature for the total yield series. It may also be useful to make other time series comparable to each other, but it probably doesn't make much sense e.g. for a quantity series.

includePendingOrders.description:
This parameter determines whether open orders should be included in the calculation or excluded from it. Open orders, which are generated in the Rebalancing, are orders that may already have been transmitted to a trading center but have yet to be confirmed.

  • true: Orders that have not been booked yet are included in the calculation. Also included are the prices at the time of the orders.
  • false: Open orders are excluded from the calculation.

The default value is false (no inclusion of open orders).

assessmentType.description:
This parameter specifies which type of quotes are requested from the provided quote supplier.

The following values are available:

  • CLOSING: The most recent closing quotes relative to the dates for which quotes are requested.
  • REAL_TIME: The most recent quotes of the supplier provided in real time.
  • NEAR_TIME: Quotes that are not provided in real time but with delay.

If nothing is selected ("-"), CLOSING is used. If no quote supplier is provided or if none of the provided quote suppliers returns a quote, the quotes are taken from the cache.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

resultMapKey.description: The key under which the result is added to the streaming data.

begin.description: This parameter specifies the beginning (inclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format.

end.description: This parameter specifies the end (exclusive) of the time period for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the YYYY-MM-DD format. It should be noted that the end date is "exclusive". If, for example, the year 2019 should be analyzed, "2019-01-01" must be specified as the starting and "2020-01-01" as the end date. An evaluation will then be made of the days 01.01.2019 up to and including 31.12.2019.

when.description: This parameter specifies the date for which the request is made. The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

aggregations.description:
The method by which the results are aggregated. Depending on the method used, the results represent different entities. For example, aggregation by POSITION leads to one result per individual position, whereas aggregation by ACCOUNT_TYPE leads to one result per separate type of account.

Multi-selection is possible. Choosing multiple aggregation methods leads to one result per combination of the chosen values. For example, aggregation by ASSET_TYPE and ACCOUNT leads to one result per combination of asset type and account that occurs in the chosen holding context.

  • PARTIAL_HOLDING: Partial holding (separation by opening and closing transaction)
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Opening transaction
  • CURRENCY: Opening transaction currency
  • QUANTITY_SIGN: Quantity sign (separation into long and short positions)
  • POSITION: Position
  • POSITION_VALUEDNESS: Position valuedness (separation into valued und unvalued positions)
  • ASSET: Asset
  • ASSET_TYPE: Asset type
  • ASSET_CURRENCY: Asset currency
  • DEPOSITORY: Depository (storage location of the financial instrument)
  • SEPARATION_KEY: Separation key (attribute for further subdivision of positions)
  • ACCOUNT: Account
  • ACCOUNT_TYPE: Account type (e.g., portfolio or settlement account)
  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

include.description:
The details the node should include in the result. Multi-selection is possible.
  • CONTRACT: Details about the contract itself.
  • PERSON: Details about all persons that are associated with the contract.
  • ORGANIZATION: Details about all organizations that are associated with the contract.
  • ACCOUNT: Details about all accounts that are associated with the contract and belong to the respective holding.
  • POSITION: Details about all positions that are associated with the contract and belong to the respective holding.
  • RESTRICTION: Details about all trading restrictions that are associated with the contract and belong to the respective holding.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.
