  • MODULE: Module
  • MODULE_TYPE: Module type (e.g., core or satellite modules)
  • CONTRACT: Portfolio
  • ALL: Selected holding context (everything is aggregated into a single holding)

propertyAggregation.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by asset properties. The selection of such properties can be configured.

esgValueAggregation.description: This parameter enables the aggregation of calculation results by esg values.    The selection of such properties can be configured.

selectedModule.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules. Not allowed if excluded modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedModule.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio module or a selection of portfolio modules from the analysis. Not allowed if selected modules are also defined.

In addition to the correct module ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified module and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedModule and excludedModule, as modules can either be only included or only excluded.

selectedAsset.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets. Not allowed if excluded assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedAsset.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset or a selection of portfolio assets from the analysis. Not allowed if selected assets are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAsset and excludedAsset, as assets can either be only included or only excluded.

selectedAssetType.description:
This parameter calculates results for a portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types. Not allowed if excluded asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

excludedAssetType.description:
This parameter excludes a single portfolio asset type or a selection of portfolio asset types from the analysis. Not allowed if selected asset types are also defined.

In addition to the correct asset ID, it is important that the field contract is correctly filled. The portfolio belonging to the specified asset and thus the correct contract ID must be entered here. It should also be noted that it is not possible to simultaneously fill both fields selectedAssetType and excludedAssetType, as asset types can either be only included or only excluded.

selectedFeeDetailedType.description:
This parameter restricts the analysis of fee details to a specific detail type, or a selection of types. Not allowed if excluded fee detail types are also defined.

Note that, differently from other selection/exclusion parameters, this parameter applies only to fee details, and has no effect on any tax details shown in the result.

excludedFeeDetailedType.description:
This parameter excludes a specific detail type, or a selection of types, from the analysis of fee details. Not allowed if selected fee detail types are also defined.

Note that, differently from other selection/exclusion parameters, this parameter applies only to fee details, and has no effect on any tax details shown in the result.

selectedTaxDetailedType.description:
This parameter restricts the analysis of tax details to a specific detail type, or a selection of types. Not allowed if excluded tax detail types are also defined.

Note that, differently from other selection/exclusion parameters, this parameter applies only to tax details, and has no effect on any fee details shown in the result.

excludedTaxDetailedType.description:
This parameter excludes a specific detail type, or a selection of types, from the analysis of tax details. Not allowed if selected tax detail types are also defined.

Note that, differently from other selection/exclusion parameters, this parameter applies only to tax details, and has no effect on any fee details shown in the result.

currencySelection.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

currency.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

paymentCalculation.description:
This parameter determines the extent to which fee details, tax details, or both are included in the calculation.

The following values are available:

  • FEES_ONLY: Includes fee details but no tax details.
  • TAXES_ONLY: Includes tax details but no fee details.
  • ALL_PAYMENTS: Includes both fee details and tax details.
The default value is "-". If no value is selected, the value ALL_PAYMENTS is used automatically.

unvaluedPositionHandling.description:
This parameter specifies the handling of unvalued positions in this request.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Both valued and unvalued positions are included in calculations.
  • EXCLUDE_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Unvalued positions are excluded from calculations.
  • SHOW_ONLY_UNVALUED_POSITIONS: Only unvalued positions are included in calculations.

If nothing is selected ("-"), NONE is used.

sourceDate.description: The date on which to determine the holdings of the original contracts.

investmentDate.description:
The date on which to create the investment transactions for the temporary contracts, based on the holdings of the original contracts.

Often, this date will lie in the past relative to the source date, but this is not a requirement.

investmentBase.description:
The characteristic of the holdings of the original contracts on which the investment transactions for the temporary contracts should be based.

The following values are available:

  • QUANTITY: Uses the holding quantities of the original contracts as the quantities of the investment transactions for the temporary contracts.
  • VALUE: Uses the holding values of the original contracts as the actual amounts of the investment transactions for the temporary contracts.

In either case, quotes on the investment date are used to calculate the other values of the investment transactions.

If not set, VALUE is used.

currencySelection.description:
How to determine the currencies in which the holdings of the original contracts are evaluated (which also determines the currencies of the associated investment transactions).

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Evaluates the holdings in the contract currency.
  • ACCOUNT: Evaluates the holdings in the account currency.
  • ASSET: Evaluates the holdings in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Evaluates the holdings in the currency of the first transaction.

If neither this parameter nor currency is set, the base currency is used.

currency.description:
The fixed currency in which the holdings of the original contracts are evaluated (which is also the fixed currency for all investment transactions).

If both this parameter and currencySelection are set, the value of currencySelection is used.

If neither this parameter nor currency is set, the base currency is used.

personConnections.description:
A classification of the persons to which the temporary contract should be connected via person-to-contract relationships.

Possible values are:

  • NONE: The temporary contract should not be connected to any persons. Be aware that this limits the temporary contract's accessibility to users with a blanket privilege such as PMS_MAY_SEE_EVERY_CONTRACT.
  • CREATOR: The temporary contract should be connected to the user that called this endpoint to create the temporary contract.
For each person to which the temporary contract should be connected, the endpoint will create one person-to-contract relationship per configured relationship type that grants access (see the preference PERSON_TO_CONTRACT_TYPES_ALLOWING_DATA_ACCESS).

If personConnections is not set, the default is taken from a preference TEMPORARY_CONTRACT_PERSON_CONNECTIONS. If this preference isn't set either, the default is CREATOR.

invalidQuoteHandling.description:
How to handle invalid quotes when creating the investment transactions for a temporary contract.

The following values are available:

  • THROW_EXCEPTION: Rejects the entire input message. No temporary contract is created, and the input message is sent to the error topic rather than the output topic.
  • SKIP_POSITION_WITH_INVALID_QUOTE: Creates the temporary contract, but doesn't create any positions for assets with invalid quotes.
  • CREATE_POSITION_WITH_INVALID_QUOTE: Creates the temporary contract, including positions for assets with invalid quotes (although the investment transactions for these positions may be incomplete).

replaceOriginalContractIds.description:
Specifies whether the original contract IDs in the input messages should be replaced with the temporary contract IDs in the contractId fields of the output messages.

If not set, the original contract IDs are kept.

originalContractIdParameterMapKey.description:
Specifies a key in the parameters map that should be used to store the original contract IDs in the output messages, in cases where the original contract IDs are replaced by temporary contract IDs in the contractId fields of the output messages.

Only relevant if keepOriginalContractIds is set to false (or not set at all). Otherwise, this parameter is ignored.

If not set, the original contract IDs are stored under the key "ORIGINAL_CONTRACT_ID".

deriveTemporaryContractIdsFromOriginals.description:
Specifies whether or not the temporary contract IDs should be derived from the original contract IDs (as opposed to using UUIDs).

If not set, random UUIDs are used as the temporary contract IDs.

derivedTemporaryContractIdPrefix.description:
Specifies a prefix for temporary contract IDs that are derived from the original contract IDs. In other words, temporary contract IDs are derived from the original contract IDs and this prefix as <prefix>:<originalContractId>.

Only relevant if deriveTemporaryContractIdsFromOriginals is set to true. Otherwise, this parameter is ignored.

If not set, "TMP" is used as a prefix.

persistence.description:
Specifies if and how to persist the temporary contracts.

The following values are available:

  • NONE: Does not persist the temporary contracts. NOTE: This means that the temporary contracts will only be available in the Analytics instance that created them. If more than one Analytics instance is running, this can lead to unpredictable errors. If the Analytics instance that created the temporary contracts crashes or is stopped, the temporary contracts are lost.
  • DATABASE: Persists the temporary contracts in the database. This is the default.
  • MESSAGE_STREAM: Persists the temporary contracts in the message stream itself by adding all necessary information to recreate the temporary contracts to the output messages. NOTE: This means that the temporary contracts may not be available outside the streaming API. If you want the temporary contracts to be reliably available for synchronous REST calls as well, you should not choose this option.

prefix.description: Specifies a prefix for temporary contract's account and position IDs that are derived from the original contract's account and position IDs. In other words, temporary contract's account and position IDs are derived from the original contract's account and position IDs and this prefix as <prefix>:<originalId>. If not set, UUIDs are used.

useProxyAssets.description: Value to determine if original assets should be replaced by proxy asset(s) based on the configured  proxy-asset-mapping criteria. If not specified, the default preference setting USE_PROXY_ASSETS  will be used to determine the behavior.

useCorporateActions.description: Determinate if corporate actions should be used on assets. If not specified, the default preferences setting USE_CORPORATE_ACTIONS will be used to determine the behavior.

reallocationInterval.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval in which the temporary contract is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested.

If at least one reallocation date (reallocationDates) is specified, this value is ignored.

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to a monthly rebalancing of the temporary contract.

This feature is still in development. For now, this parameter does not do anything!

reallocationDates.description:
This parameter specifies the exact times at which the temporary contract is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested.

The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any number of dates can be specified.

This feature is still in development. For now, this parameter does not do anything!

restoreOriginalContractIds.description:
Specifies whether or not the original contract IDs should be restored to the contractId fields of the output messages after deleting the temporary contracts.

This requires the original contract IDs to be present in the parameters map of the input messages. Note that if they are present, but use a different key than "ORIGINAL_CONTRACT_ID", the key has to be specified via originalContractIdParameterMapKey.

If not set, the original contract IDs are not restored in the output messages.

originalContractIdParameterMapKey.description:
Specifies a key in the parameters map that should be used to retrieve the original contract IDs from the input messages, in order to restore them to the contractId fields of the output messages.

Only relevant if restoreOriginalContractIds is set to true. Otherwise, this parameter is ignored.

If not set, the original contract IDs are expected to be found under the key "ORIGINAL_CONTRACT_ID".

persistenceKey.description: This is the id, the data will be stored with. This enables receiving the data via REST by only passing this reasonId to the corresponding GET endpoint.

persistenceData.description: This parameter specifies the data that should be extracted from the results map in ContractStreamingData and will be persisted to the database.

persistenceParams.description: This parameter specifies the data that should be extracted from the parameters map in ContractStreamingData and will be persisted to the database.

holdingId.description: The aggregation parameters that identify the holding.

quantity.description:
The quantity of the holding on the given date.

Included only if the aggregation methods include at least one of POSITION and/or ASSET.

value.description: The value on the given date, expressed in the selected currency.

accruedInterest.description: The accrued interest on the given date, expressed in the selected currency.

valueAndAccruedInterest.description: The sum of value and accrued interest on the given date, expressed in the selected currency.

relativeValue.description:
The value portion on the given date, relative to the total value of all selected contracts.

Included only if the includeTotals flag is set to true.

relativeAccruedInterest.description:
The accrued interest portion on the given date, relative to the total accrued interest of all selected contracts.

Included only if the includeTotals flag is set to true.

relativeValueAndAccruedInterest.description:
The value and accrued interest portion on the given date, relative to the total value and accrued interest of all selected contracts.

Included only if the includeTotals flag is set to true.

ContractsOverviewValue.description: The absolute and relative value and accrued interest of the holding on the given date.

initialCost.description: The initial cost on the given date.

initialPrice.description: The initial price on the given date.

initialCurrencyPrice.description: The initial currency price on the given date.

ContractsOverviewInitialValue.description: The initial values of the holding on the given date.

ContractsOverviewValuation.description:
Valuation data for the holding on the given date.

Included only if the aggregation methods include at least one of POSITION and/or ASSET.

ContractsOverviewValuationData.description: The valuation of the holding on the given date.

ContractsOverviewValuationGap.description: The valuation gap of the holding, if any, on the given date.

firstInvestmentDate.description: The date of the oldest transaction that contributed to the holding.

lastInvestmentDate.description: The date of the youngest transaction that contributed to the holding.

overallDataQuality.description: The worst data quality of all transactions that contributed to the holding.

ContractsOverviewInvestments.description:
The investments (purchase and committal transactions) that contributed to the holding on the given date.

Note that this is not a complete transaction history. It doesn't contain sale or delivery transactions, since by definition they didn't contribute to whatever is left afterwards. Similarly, it doesn't contain purchase or committal transactions whose quantity was completely sold before the given date, and it may contain only portions of purchase and committal transactions that did contribute to the holding in case of partial sales.

ContractsOverviewInvestment.description:
One investment (purchase or committal transaction) that contributed to the holding on the given date.

Note that this may only be a portion of the original transaction if a partial sale happened afterwards.

priceBasedReturnRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss caused by quote fluctuations.

priceBasedReturnUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss caused by quote fluctuations.

priceBasedReturn.description: The total gain or loss caused by quote fluctuations.

currencyBasedReturnRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss caused by exchange rate fluctuations.

currencyBasedReturnUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss caused by exchange rate fluctuations.

currencyBasedReturn.description: The total gain or loss caused by exchange rate fluctuations.

accruedInterestRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss due to accrued interest.

accruedInterestUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss due to accrued interest.

accruedInterest.description: The total gain or loss due to accrued interest.

creditAdvicesRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss due to credit advices.

creditAdvicesUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss due to credit advices.

creditAdvices.description: The total gain or loss due to credit advices.

feesRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss due to fees.

feesUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss due to fees.

fees.description: The total gain or loss due to fees.

taxesRealized.description: The realized portion of any gain or loss due to taxes.

taxesUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of any gain or loss due to taxes.

taxes.description: The total gain or loss due to taxes.

totalReturnRealized.description: The realized portion of the overall gain or loss of the holding.

totalReturnUnrealized.description: The unrealized portion of the overall gain or loss of the holding.

totalReturn.description: The overall gain or loss of the holding.

averageInvestment.description: The average investment of the holding.

ContractsOverviewReturn.description: The total return of the holding from its first investment date to the given date, as well as the components (such as price-based return, currency-based return etc.) that make up this total.

modifiedDietzPerformance.description: The modified Dietz performance of the holding.

moneyWeightedPerformance.description: The money-weighted performance of the holding.

timeWeightedPerformance.description: The time-weighted performance of the holding.

ContractsOverviewPerformance.description: The performance of the holding from its first investment date to the given date.

ContractsOverviewAsset.description:
All selected information about the holding's asset.

Included only if the aggregation methods include at least one of POSITION and/or ASSET.

ContractsOverviewAssetBasicData.description: Basic information about the holding's asset.

relatedAssets.description: The list of all assets that the holding's asset is related to, if any.

underlyingAssets.description: The list of all underlying assets of the holding's asset, if it has any.

composingAssets.description: The list of all assets from which the holding's asset is constructed, if any.

ContractsOverviewAssetRelationships.description: All relationships to other assets that the holding's asset has on the given date.

ContractsOverviewAssetProperties.description: All properties that the holding's asset has on the given date.

propertyType.description: The type of all properties included in this list.

begin.description: The date on which the holding's asset acquired these properties.

end.description: The date on which the holding's asset will lose these properties.

properties.description: The list of all properties of the given type that the holding's asset has on the given date.

ContractsOverviewAssetPropertyProportion.description: All properties of a specific type that the holding's asset has on the given date.
A single property that the holding's asset has on the given date.

ContractsOverviewAssetQuote.description:
The best available quote in the selected currency for the holding's asset on the given date.

Also contains the exchange rate between the quote's currency and the base currency if the selected currency is not the base currency.

ContractsOverviewAssetQuoteData.description: The best available quote or exchange rate for the holding's asset on the given date.

begin.description: The date on which the holding's asset acquired these interest rates.

end.description: The date on which the holding's asset will lose these interest rates.

interestRates.description: The list of all interest rates that the holding's asset has on the given date.

The interest rate(s) of the holding's asset on the given date.

In most cases, this is just one interest rate, but in case of quantity-dependent interest, there may be several.

lastInterestDate.description: The last interest date of the holding's asset before the given date.

ContractsOverviewAssetInterest.description:
The interest rate(s) of the holding's asset on the given date, if any.

Also contains the last interest date of the holding's asset before the given date, if any.

ContractsOverviewAssetDividend.description: The last dividend paid for the holding's asset on or before the given date.

ContractsOverviewAssetInterestRate.description: One of the interest rates of the holding's asset on the given date.

ContractsOverviewDerivative.description: Information about the given derivative including available risks on the given date.

PersistedContractStreamingData.description: The results fields to be persisted during streaming.

PersistedContractStreamingDataParameters.description: The parameter fields to be persisted during streaming.

currencyId.description: The currency of the investment's monetary value.

amount.description: The monetary amount to invest in the asset.

shortName.description: A short name or description for the property.

longName.description: A longer name or description for the property.

value.description:
The property's numerical value, if it has one.

Relevant for calculating average properties.

parent.description: The ID of the property's parent property, if it has one.

assetId.description: Specifies the asset in which to invest. Note that this has to be a real asset that exists in the  Analytics data.

accountId.description: Specifies the account in which to invest. Note that this must be a unique ID that does not exist  as a real account ID, and isn’t used in any other temporary contracts either. If not set, a random  unique ID is used. Note that all investments that don’t specify an account ID are assigned to the  same account within the temporary contract.

depositoryId.description: Specifies the depository to which to assign the investment. Note that this has to be a real  depository that exists in the Analytics data. If not set, the investment is not assigned to a  depository.

separationKey.description: Specifies the separation key to which to assign the investment. Note that this has to be a real  separation key that exists in the Analytics data. If not set, the investment is not assigned to a  separation key.

positionId.description: Specifies the position in which to invest. Note that this must be a unique ID that does not exist  as a real position ID, and isn’t used in any other temporary contracts either. If not set, a random  unique ID is used. Note that all investments with the same combination of account ID, asset ID,  depository ID, and separation key must belong to the same position. If you specify a position ID  for some investments with a given combination of account ID, asset ID, depository ID, and separation  key but not for others, the specified position ID (rather than a random unique ID) is used for all  investments with this combination.

quantity.description: The quantity to invest in the asset. Three combinations with value are possible: When both parameters are given, then the quote for the transaction is calculated. If only quantity is given, then the quote is looked up. While value without quantity uses the default value of 1.

currency.description: The currency in which to make the investment.

contractId.description: An optional but unique identifier for the temporary contract. If omitted, the system generates  one automatically. If the ID matches an existing temporary contract, that contract will be  overwritten with the new data. The response always includes the final ID used.

when.description: The start date of the simulation. It should be before the source date or the date of the API  request. But this is not enforced.

personConnections.description:
A classification of the persons to which the temporary contract should be connected via person-to-contract relationships.

Possible values are:

  • NONE: The temporary contract should not be connected to any persons. Be aware that this limits the temporary contract's accessibility to users with a blanket privilege such as PMS_MAY_SEE_EVERY_CONTRACT.
  • CREATOR: The temporary contract should be connected to the user that called this endpoint to create the temporary contract.
For each person to which the temporary contract should be connected, the endpoint will create one person-to-contract relationship per configured relationship type that grants access (see the preference PERSON_TO_CONTRACT_TYPES_ALLOWING_DATA_ACCESS).

If personConnections is not set, the default is taken from a preference TEMPORARY_CONTRACT_PERSON_CONNECTIONS. If this preference isn't set either, the default is CREATOR.

invalidQuoteHandling.description: Value to determine how invalid quotes are handled. This value can be THROW_EXCEPTION,  SKIP_POSITION_WITH_INVALID_QUOTE or CREATE_POSITION_WITH_INVALID_QUOTE. If left empty the  configured default will be used.

useProxyAssets.description: Value to determine if an original asset should be replaced by a proxy asset. The replacement follows  rules defined in a separate proxy_asset_mapping configuration. If not specified, the default  preference setting USE_PROXY_ASSETS is used. If this is also not defined, the default value is  FALSE.

useCorporateActions.description: Determinate if corporate actions should be used on assets. If not specified, the default preferences setting USE_CORPORATE_ACTIONS will be used to determine the behavior.

reallocationInterval.description:
This parameter sets the regular time interval in which the temporary contract is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested.

If at least one reallocation date (reallocationDates) is specified, this value is ignored.

The input value is an integer, for example, a value of 30 corresponds to a monthly rebalancing of the temporary contract.

This feature is still in development. For now, this parameter does not do anything!

reallocationDates.description:
This parameter specifies the exact times at which the temporary contract is rebalanced and any gains that may have been incurred are reinvested.

The date must be entered in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any number of dates can be specified.

This feature is still in development. For now, this parameter does not do anything!

sourceContractIds.description: A list of contract IDs on which to base the temporary contract. Either a list of contract IDs or a  contract set has to be provided but not both.

sourceContractSet.description: A contract set on which to base the temporary contract. A list of contract IDs and  a contract set should be mutually exclusive, but providing one of them is required.

sourceDate.description: The date on which to determine the holdings of the given contract(s).  If not specified, the current date is used.

investmentBase.description:
The holding characteristic on which the investments of the temporary contract should be based. If not set, VALUE is used. The following options are available:
  • QUANTITY: The investments of the temporary contract will be based on the asset quantities of the source contracts on the source date.
  • VALUE: The investments of the temporary contract will be based on the asset values of the source contracts on the source date.

currencySelection.description:
This parameter specifies the currency in which results are shown. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the value of currencySelection is used. If neither parameter is set, the base currency is used. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation of the performance.

The following values are available:

  • CONTRACT: Shows the result in the portfolio currency.
  • ASSET: Shows the result in the asset currency.
  • OPENING_TRANSACTION: Shows the result in the currency of the first transaction.
  • ACCOUNT: Shows the result in the account currency.

If nothing is selected ("-"), the calculation is made in the configured base currency.

currency.description:
This parameter determines the currency in which calculation results are shown. It should be noted that positive or negative currency effects may affect the calculation results.

Currency abbreviations are represented as published by the International Organization for Standardization in the ISO 4217 as a reference for accurately identifying currencies.

This parameter can only be selected, if the parameter currencySelection is empty or if "-" is displayed. If both currencySelection and currency are set, the calculation is made in the selected currency of the parameter currencySelection.

prefix.description: Specifies a prefix for temporary contract's account and position IDs that are derived from the original contract's account and position IDs. In other words, temporary contract's account and position IDs are derived from the original contract's account and position IDs and this prefix as <prefix>:<originalId>. If not set, UUIDs are used.

detail.example: Invalid Position Valuation Type : VALUATE. Allowed values for Position Valuation Type: [ALL, VALUATED, UNVALUATED]
User does not have valid credentials to access the service resource.
User does not have required permissions to access the service resource.
Service unavailable possibly due to unavailability of Caches.

assets.description:
The requested assets.

If any assets could not be found based on the request parameters, they are left out of this list.

assetIsin.description: The asset's ISIN, if it has one.

assetLocalId.description: The asset's local ID, if it has one.

assetShortName.description: A short name or description for the asset.

assetLongName.description: A longer name or description for the asset.

propertyType.description: The type of the properties in this entry.

properties.description: The asset's properties of the specified type.

propertyShortName.description: A short name or description for the property.

propertyLongName.description: A longer name or description for the property.

propertyValue.description:
The property's numerical value, if it has one.

Relevant for calculating average properties.

propertyProportion.description:
The property's proportion among all of the asset's properties of the same type.

In most cases, an asset will only have one property of any given type. In these cases, the proportion is 1. Otherwise, the proportions of all properties of the same type add up to 1.

Note that some proportions can be negative, but even then they still add up to 1. (Which also means proportions can be greater than 1, if they're balanced by a negative proportion.)

propertyProportionBegin.description:
The date on which the property's proportion became valid.

If an asset's property proportions change over time, the proportions shown here are the ones that are valid on the reference date given in the request.

For any given asset, all properties of the same type have the same property proportion begin date.

parentPropertyId.description: The ID of the property's parent property, if it has one.

childPropertyType.description: The type of the property's child properties, if it has any.

childProperties.description: The property's child properties, if it has any.
